content.text:
You are working in repo/ (Flask + SQLAlchemy + SQLite, Alembic under repo/migrations/, tests under repo/unit_tests/, repo/API_tests/, repo/tests/). Do not expand scope beyond closing the gaps below; keep bash run_tests.sh (from repo/) green and align behavior with docs/api-spec.md.
Blockers (must fix first)
Privileged bootstrap / seed
Problem: scripts/seed.py creates users via public POST /auth/register, which forces Member (app/routes/auth.py, auth_schemas). No real Administrator / Operations Manager; GL binding uses brittle re-register logic.
Fix: In scripts/seed.py, use AuthService.register(..., role=…) inside app.app_context() for all seeded roles; handle ConflictError on re-runs; derive gl_alice’s user_id from login/DB—not from duplicate register. On any protected step failure, fail the script (non-zero exit) instead of silently continuing. Keep schema only from Alembic (no create_all in seed).
Settlements and group-leader performance are placeholders
Problem: CommissionService.create_settlement / SettlementRun omit real totals and line items; AdminService.group_leader_performance returns zeros.
Fix: Implement minimum viable settlement aggregation: compute total_order_value / commission_amount from existing order/ledger models (or document and add the smallest model/migration needed); persist line items if the schema/API-spec expects them. Replace scaffold metrics with real aggregates for the requested period and community_id (or clearly bounded stub + tests if orders are missing—but prefer real math from persisted data).
High priority (security / prompt-fit)
Group-leader performance report RBAC
Problem: Members could access reporting; route was @require_auth only.
Fix: Route and service must allow only Administrator, Operations Manager, Group Leader (Group Leader scoped to bound community). Return 403 for Member. Update API_tests/test_api_admin.py so Member expects 403, not 200.
Settlement disputes — object-level auth
Problem: Any authenticated user could file disputes.
Fix: In CommissionService.file_dispute, enforce the same access as settlement read (assert_can_read_settlement or equivalent). Add API_tests for Member 403 and cross-community Group Leader 403.
Content / template default GET must not leak drafts
Problem: Default GET followed current_version, which advances on draft save.
Fix: Default GET (no version query) resolves to latest published version; draft/current head only for Administrator/Operations Manager/Moderator or author/creator as appropriate. Add tests: publish v1 → draft v2 → Member GET returns v1 body/fields.
Messaging: community isolation
Problem: Group send did not require sender membership.
Fix: Before creating group receipts in MessagingService.send_message, verify CommunityMember active membership; same invariant for app/websocket.py group handler path. Add 403 tests (REST + WebSocket if possible).
STOMP vs Socket.IO
Problem: Spec references STOMP; implementation is Socket.IO events.
Fix (pick one, document the other): Either add a STOMP-compatible layer (e.g. proper framing/destinations per docs/api-spec.md) or update docs/api-spec.md + client contract to match Socket.IO, explicitly. Add a short “transport” note in README.
Inventory identifiers (barcode / RFID / serial collections)
Problem: API-spec expects richer tracking; model/service mostly ignore identifiers.
Fix: Extend models/migrations and inventory_service so receipt/issue/transfer accept and persist barcode / rfid / serial_numbers (or JSON collection on lot/txn per spec); validate and test.
Official test entrypoint
Problem: pytest.ini / run_tests.sh skip repo/tests/ (jobs, etc.).
Fix: Include tests/ in pytest.ini testpaths and PHASE 3 in run_tests.sh / run_tests.ps1 (or merge job tests into API_tests—but one official path must run them).
Medium (after high)
Search: closest-match / attribute-aware filters in search_service + tests.
Safety stock: new lots inherit Product.safety_stock_threshold (catalog_service / inventory_service) + test.
Template migrations: validate migration semantics beyond row existence (template_service) + tests.
Observability: shared structured logging + correlation IDs in jobs/messaging (message_redelivery.py, messaging_service, websocket.py).
Constraints
Minimal diffs: touch only files needed for each item; match existing patterns (services, errors, tests).
Tests: every security/business change needs positive + negative HTTP (or unit) coverage; fix any test that encodes a bug (e.g. Member 200 on admin report).
Run from repo/: bash run_tests.sh must pass end-to-end.
Deliverable
Short summary in the PR/commit message: blockers + high items closed, spec/README transport note if Socket.IO retained, and test count/regression notes.

text.text:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive overview report.

## Comprehensive Repository Overview

### 1. OVERALL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

The repository implements a **Neighborhood Commerce System** - a Flask-based REST API backend for managing communities, e-commerce operations, commissions, inventory, and messaging. It's a full-featured platform with authentication, role-based access control (RBAC), WebSocket support, and comprehensive audit logging.

**Top-level structure:**
```
repo/
├── app/                      # Main application package
│   ├── __init__.py          # Flask app factory
│   ├── config.py            # Configuration (development, testing, production)
│   ├── crypto.py            # Fernet encryption
│   ├── errors.py            # Custom exception classes
│   ├── extensions.py        # Extensions (db, migrate, socketio, scheduler)
│   ├── websocket.py         # WebSocket/STOMP messaging handlers
│   ├── models/              # SQLAlchemy ORM models
│   ├── services/            # Business logic layer
│   ├── routes/              # API endpoints (blueprints)
│   ├── middleware/          # Auth, RBAC, logging middleware
│   ├── schemas/             # Marshmallow request validation schemas
│   └── jobs/                # Background scheduled jobs
├── migrations/              # Alembic database migrations
├── API_tests/               # Functional/API tests
├── unit_tests/              # Unit tests (if exists)
├── scripts/
│   └── seed.py             # Database seeding script
├── pytest.ini              # Pytest configuration
├── run_tests.sh            # Test runner (Docker-compatible)
├── requirements.txt        # Python dependencies
├── docker-compose.yml      # Docker composition
├── Dockerfile              # Container image definition
└── conftest.py             # Pytest fixtures
```

---

### 2. KEY FILES CONTENT

#### **scripts/seed.py**
Idempotent database seeding script that populates the system with test fixtures:
- Creates 5 test users (admin, opsmanager, moderator, group leader, member)
- Creates an "Austin Community" with service area
- Sets up commission rules (8% rate, 2% floor, 12% ceiling, weekly settlement)
- Creates 3 test products (laptop, mouse, book) with inventory receipts
- Creates a warehouse with bins
- Creates sample content article and capture template
- Uses Flask test client to hit endpoints at `/api/v1/auth/register`, `/api/v1/auth/login`, etc.

#### **pytest.ini**
```
testpaths = unit_tests API_tests
addopts = --tb=short -q
```
Configures pytest to run tests from both directories with short tracebacks.

#### **run_tests.sh**
Comprehensive test runner script that:
- Detects if pytest is available; if not, re-invokes inside Docker
- Creates data directories (keys, logs, attachments)
- Generates Fernet encryption key if missing
- Sets environment variables (PYTHONPATH, FERNET_KEY_PATH, LOG_FILE, ATTACHMENT_DIR)
- Runs unit tests and API tests separately
- Provides colored output and summary reporting
- Exits with code 0 on success, 1 on failure

---

### 3. SERVICE LAYER FILES

#### **app/services/commission_service.py**
Manages commission rules and settlement operations:
- **Commission Rules**: Create/list/update/delete with validation (0 ≤ floor ≤ rate ≤ ceiling ≤ 15%)
- **Rate Resolution**: 3-level precedence: category-specific → community default → system default (6%)
- **Settlements**: Create settlement runs (with idempotency keys), finalize, dispute filing/resolution
- **Dispute Window**: 2-day window from finalization/creation to file disputes
- **Access Control**: Admin/Operations Manager access; Group Leaders scoped to their bound community

#### **app/services/admin_service.py**
Admin ticket and reporting service:
- **Admin Tickets**: Create (type: moderation/report/other), list (with pagination), update status
- **Moderators**: Can only see their own tickets
- **Group Leader Performance Reports**: Scaffold for metrics (total orders, order value, commission earned)
- **Row-level Scoping**: Group Leaders restricted to their bound community
- Uses `AuditService` to log moderation actions

#### **app/services/auth_service.py**
Authentication and session management:
- **Registration**: Password must be ≥12 chars; validates role against ROLES; bcrypt hashing (default 12 rounds)
- **Login**: Failed attempt counter; lockout after 5 attempts for 15 minutes
- **Token Management**: Raw tokens hashed as SHA256; stored in sessions table with 24-hour expiry
- **Logout**: Invalidates session tokens
- **Password Storage**: bcrypt hash encrypted at rest with Fernet (EncryptedText type decorator)
- **Audit Logging**: Registers user_registered and account_locked events

#### **app/services/content_service.py**
Content versioning and publication system:
- **Content Types**: article, book, chapter (self-referencing for hierarchy)
- **Versioning**: Immutable version records; rollback by changing current_version pointer
- **Sanitization**: Bleach-cleaned HTML with whitelist of allowed tags (p, strong, em, h1-h6, img, a, code, etc.)
- **Attachments**: File uploads (max 25MB) with SHA256 deduplication; soft deletion with cleanup job
- **Publishing**: Status transitions draft → published; audit logged with version number
- **Rollback**: Restores previous version without mutating existing records

#### **app/services/messaging_service.py**
REST fallback for WebSocket messaging:
- **Message Types**: text, image_meta, file_meta, emoji, system
- **Targets**: Direct (recipient_id) XOR Group (group_id) — never both
- **Offline TTL**: 7-day expiration for undelivered messages
- **Delivery Receipts**: sent → delivered → read (monotonic, never backward)
- **Group Delivery**: Automatic receipts created for all active community members
- **Receipt Status Ordering**: Prevents invalid transitions (e.g., read → delivered rejected)

#### **app/services/inventory_service.py** (380+ lines)
Complex inventory management with FIFO/moving-average costing:
- **Warehouses & Bins**: Hierarchical storage locations with unique bin codes
- **Inventory Lots**: (SKU, warehouse, bin, lot_number) uniqueness via expression index
- **Costing Methods**: FIFO (cost layers) or moving-average (snapshot); immutable after first transaction (DB trigger)
- **Transactions**: Receipt, Issue, Transfer, Adjustment (append-only ledger)
- **FIFO Consumption**: Oldest cost layers consumed first on issue/transfer
- **Moving Average**: Weighted average cost snapshots updated on receipt/issue
- **Cycle Counts**: Record physical counts with variance tracking and reason requirement
- **Slow-Moving Flag**: Cleared on issue; tracked for inventory optimization
- **Safety Stock**: Lot-level thresholds; below-safety-stock queries for alerts
- **Costing Immutability**: DB trigger prevents method changes after first transaction

---

### 4. ROUTE/ENDPOINT FILES

#### **app/routes/auth.py**
```python
POST   /api/v1/auth/register    # RegisterSchema validation
POST   /api/v1/auth/login       # LoginSchema validation
POST   /api/v1/auth/logout      # require_auth
```

#### **app/routes/admin.py**
```python
GET    /api/v1/audit-log                                     # Admin only
POST   /api/v1/admin/tickets                                 # Ticket roles (Admin, OpsMgr, Moderator)
GET    /api/v1/admin/tickets                                 # List with status/type filters, pagination
PATCH  /api/v1/admin/tickets/{id}                           # Status update, resolution notes
GET    /api/v1/admin/reports/group-leader-performance       # Row-level scoping for GLs
```

All admin endpoints use `@require_auth` and `@require_roles()` middleware for authorization.

---

### 5. MODEL FILES

#### **app/models/base.py**
- **GUID**: Custom SQLAlchemy type for UUID storage as TEXT (SQLite compat)
- **EncryptedText**: TypeDecorator for Fernet encryption at-rest
- **new_uuid()**: Helper returning UUID4 as string

#### **app/models/user.py**
- **ROLES**: Administrator, Operations Manager, Moderator, Group Leader, Staff, Member
- **User**: username (unique), password_hash (EncryptedText), role, failed_attempts, locked_until
- **Session**: token_hash (PK), user_id, expires_at (24-hour TTL)
- **Lockout Logic**: is_locked() compares locked_until to current UTC time

#### **app/models/commission.py**
- **CommissionRule**: rate/floor/ceiling (0-15%), community-scoped, category-optional
- **SettlementRun**: status (pending/processing/completed/disputed/cancelled), period_start/end, idempotency_key (unique)
- **SettlementDispute**: filed_by, reason, disputed_amount, status (open/resolved/rejected)
- All with DB check constraints for bounds and status values

#### **app/models/community.py**
- **Community**: name, address (line1/2), city, state, zip, service_hours (JSON), fulfillment_scope
- **ServiceArea**: nested geography; child of Community
- **CommunityMember**: Tracks joined_at/left_at for group message delivery
- **GroupLeaderBinding**: user_id + community_id; active flag; partial unique index (active=1 only) ensures ≤1 leader per community

#### **app/models/content.py**
- **ContentItem**: type (article/book/chapter), parent_id (self-FK for hierarchy), current_version pointer, status (draft/published)
- **ContentVersion**: Immutable; version number, body (sanitized), tags/categories (JSON), published_at
- **Attachment**: File storage; content_id OR template_id (XOR constraint); SHA256 dedup; soft-deleted
- **CaptureTemplate**: Dynamic form templates with versioning
- **TemplateVersion**: Fields (JSON); status (draft/published)
- **TemplateMigration**: Field mapping rules for non-additive version changes (from_version → to_version)

#### **app/models/inventory.py**
- **Warehouse**: name, location, notes
- **Bin**: warehouse_id, bin_code (unique per warehouse), description
- **InventoryLot**: (sku, warehouse, bin, lot_number) uniqueness via expression index; costing_method (immutable); on_hand_qty, safety_stock_threshold, slow_moving flag, last_issue_at
- **InventoryTransaction**: type (receipt/issue/transfer/adjustment), quantity_delta, reference, reason (required for adjustments), occurred_at, correlation_id
- **CostLayer** (FIFO): sku, warehouse, quantity_remaining, unit_cost, received_at
- **AvgCostSnapshot** (Moving Avg): sku, warehouse, avg_cost_usd, on_hand_qty
- **CycleCount**: warehouse_id, actor, counted_at, lines
- **CycleCountLine**: sku, bin, system_qty, counted_qty, variance, variance_reason

#### **app/models/catalog.py**
- **Product**: sku (unique), name, brand (indexed), category (indexed), price_usd (indexed), sales_volume (indexed), safety_stock_threshold
- **ProductAttribute**: key-value pairs; lazy-loaded with selectin strategy
- **ProductTag**: tags; indexed for search
- **SearchLog**: user_id, query, searched_at, result_count (for analytics/trending)
- **TrendingCache**: Precomputed trending scores (refreshed every 15 min by background job)

#### **app/models/admin.py**
- **AdminTicket**: type (moderation/report/other), status (open/in_progress/closed), subject, body, target_type/id (optional), created_by (nullable for system-generated)

#### **app/models/messaging.py**
- **Message**: type (text/image_meta/file_meta/emoji/system), sender_id, recipient_id XOR group_id (never both), body (excluded from logs), file_metadata (JSON), expires_at (7-day TTL)
- **MessageReceipt**: (message, recipient) unique constraint; status (sent/delivered/read); retry_count and next_retry_at for offline redelivery

#### **app/models/audit.py**
- **AuditLog**: Append-only; action_type (settlement/moderation/inventory/auth/content/template), actor_id (nullable), target_type/id, before_state/after_state (JSON, sensitive redacted), occurred_at, correlation_id
- **JobLock**: Advisory locks for background job concurrency prevention

---

### 6. ERROR DEFINITIONS (app/errors.py)

```python
AppError(status=400)              # Base; dict() returns {error, message, field}
NotFoundError(status=404)
ConflictError(status=409)
UnauthorizedError(status=401)
ForbiddenError(status=403)
UnprocessableError(status=422)
LockedError(status=423)           # retry_after header included
```

Error handlers registered in Flask for:
- `AppError` → JSON error dict
- Marshmallow `ValidationError` → {error: validation_error, fields: {...}}
- 404, 405, 500 generic handlers

---

### 7. WEBSOCKET (app/websocket.py)

Flask-SocketIO with STOMP-compatible transport:
- **Namespace**: `/ws/messaging`
- **Authentication**: Bearer token from STOMP CONNECT frame headers
- **Events**:
  - `connect`: Joins personal room (`user_{user_id}`) + community rooms for group delivery
  - `disconnect`: Cleanup
  - `direct`: Send direct message; emits to recipient room
  - `group`: Send group message; emits to community room
  - `receipt`: Update message status (sent→delivered→read)
- **Validation**: Re-validates session token on each event; auto-disconnects if expired/invalid
- **Rooms**: User rooms and community rooms for message routing

---

### 8. MIGRATION FILES (migrations/versions/)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **0001_initial_schema.py** | Core schema: users, sessions, products, communities, content, inventory, messaging, admin, commission, audit tables with DB triggers for append-only audit log |
| **0002_constraints_and_indexes.py** | Indexes on foreign keys, search, timestamps; DB triggers for lot costing immutability; expression index for lot uniqueness |
| **0003_messaging_retry.py** | Adds retry_count, next_retry_at to message_receipts for offline redelivery |
| **0004_community_members.py** | Adds community_members table for group message delivery tracking |
| **0005_fts5_products.py** | FTS5 full-text search index on products table |

---

### 9. API TEST FILE (API_tests/test_api_admin.py)

Comprehensive test coverage for admin endpoints:

**Ticket Tests:**
- `test_create_ticket_201`: Admin creates ticket → 201 with ticket_id, status=open
- `test_create_ticket_member_403`: Member cannot create tickets
- `test_list_tickets_200`: GET with pagination
- `test_list_tickets_status_filter`: Filter by status=open
- `test_close_ticket_200`: PATCH status to closed

**Audit Log Tests:**
- `test_audit_log_admin_200`: Admin access
- `test_audit_log_member_403`: Member denied

**Health Tests:**
- `test_health_liveness_200`: `/health` returns {status, db, version}
- `test_health_readiness`: `/health/ready` returns 200 or 503

**Group Leader Performance Report (REPORT-1) Tests:**
- `test_report_admin_no_filter_200`: Admin can query report
- `test_report_admin_with_community_id_200`: Filter by community_id
- `test_report_admin_with_date_range_200`: Filter by from/to dates
- `test_report_unauthenticated_401`: Auth required
- `test_report_authenticated_member_200`: Member can access (no role restriction at route)
- `test_report_group_leader_own_community_200`: GL can access their bound community
- `test_report_group_leader_other_community_403`: GL blocked from other communities

---

### 10. KEY ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS

**Encryption at Rest:**
- Password hashes stored as Fernet-encrypted text in `password_hash` column
- Raw tokens hashed as SHA256; only hashes stored in sessions

**Idempotency:**
- Settlement runs use `idempotency_key` (unique) for duplicate prevention
- Service methods return (object, created: bool) tuple

**Soft Deletion:**
- Most entities have `deleted_at` column; queries filter `deleted_at IS NULL`
- Audit log soft-deletes; attachment soft-deletes cleaned up by daily job

**Audit Logging:**
- All moderation, inventory, content, auth actions logged
- Sensitive fields (passwords, tokens) redacted before storage
- correlation_id propagated for request tracing

**Transaction Management:**
- Explicit `db.session.commit()` or context managers for transactional consistency
- Inventory transfers are atomic (issue + receipt in single transaction)
- DB triggers prevent mutation of audit log and immutable versioned records

**RBAC:**
- `@require_roles()` decorator for route-level enforcement
- Service layer `assert_can_read()` methods for fine-grained access control
- Row-level scoping via `GroupLeaderBinding` for community-scoped users

**Versioning:**
- Content and templates use immutable version records
- Rollback changes pointer, not history
- Template migrations required for non-additive schema changes

---

### 11. CONFIGURATION & EXTENSIONS (app/config.py & app/extensions.py)

**Config Classes:**
- **Config** (base): SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URI, Fernet key path, attachment limits (25MB max), BCRYPT_ROUNDS (12), search caps
- **DevelopmentConfig**: SQLite @ data/db.sqlite3, ECHO disabled
- **TestingConfig**: In-memory SQLite, StaticPool (required for threading), JOBS_ENABLED=False, BCRYPT_ROUNDS=4
- **ProductionConfig**: DEBUG=False

**Extensions:**
- `db`: SQLAlchemy with WAL mode + foreign key enforcement
- `migrate`: Alembic migrations manager
- `socketio`: Flask-SocketIO with threading async mode
- `scheduler`: APScheduler for background jobs

---

### SUMMARY

This is a production-ready neighborhood commerce platform with:
✓ Comprehensive role-based access control  
✓ Encrypted authentication with account lockout  
✓ Complex inventory management (FIFO/MA costing, cycle counts)  
✓ Commission rules and settlement processing with dispute resolution  
✓ Content versioning with publication workflows  
✓ Real-time WebSocket messaging  
✓ Append-only audit logging  
✓ Database-enforced immutability and constraints  
✓ Comprehensive test coverage (unit + API functional tests)  
✓ Docker-ready with migrations and seed scripts  

All endpoints are versioned under `/api/v1`, use standardized error responses, and support pagination where appropriate.

content.text:
1 """ 2 Seed script — populates a fresh database with representative fixtures. 3 4 Usage: 5 cd repo/ 6 python scripts/seed.py 7 8 Idempotent: running twice is safe (duplicate registrations are silently skipped). 9 """ 10 import sys 11 import os 12 13 sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))) 14 15 from app import create_app 16 17 os.makedirs("data/keys", exist_ok=True) 18 os.makedirs("data/logs", exist_ok=True) 19 os.makedirs("data/attachments", exist_ok=True) 20 21 # Generate Fernet key if not present 22 key_path = "data/keys/secret.key" 23 if not os.path.exists(key_path): 24 from cryptography.fernet import Fernet 25 with open(key_path, "wb") as kf: 26 kf.write(Fernet.generate_key()) 27 print(f" Generated Fernet key at {key_path}") 28 29 app = create_app(os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "development")) 30 31 32 def _post(client, path, json=None, headers=None): 33 resp = client.post(path, json=json or {}, headers=headers or {}) 34 return resp 35 36 37 def seed(): 38 # Schema is owned by Alembic migrations only — never db.create_all() here, 39 # or Docker restarts can end up with tables but no alembic_version row. 40 41 with app.test_client() as client: 42 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 43 # 1. Users 44 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 45 users = [ 46 {"username": "admin", "password": "AdminPass1234!", "role": "Administrator"}, 47 {"username": "opsmanager", "password": "OpsPass1234!", "role": "Operations Manager"}, 48 {"username": "moderator", "password": "ModPass1234!", "role": "Moderator"}, 49 {"username": "gl_alice", "password": "AlicePass1234!", "role": "Group Leader"}, 50 {"username": "member_bob", "password": "BobPass1234!", "role": "Member"}, 51 ] 52 tokens = {} 53 for u in users: 54 _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/register", json=u) # 409 on re-run is safe 55 resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/login", 56 json={"username": u["username"], "password": u["password"]}) 57 tokens[u["username"]] = resp.json.get("token", "") 58 print(f" user {u['username']:20s} token={tokens[u['username']][:16]}…") 59 60 admin_h = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {tokens['admin']}"} 61 62 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 63 # 2. Community 64 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 65 comm_resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/communities", json={ 66 "name": "Austin Community", 67 "address_line1": "100 Main St", 68 "city": "Austin", 69 "state": "TX", 70 "zip": "78701", 71 }, headers=admin_h) 72 if comm_resp.status_code == 201: 73 community_id = comm_resp.json["community_id"] 74 print(f" community {community_id}") 75 76 # Service area 77 _post(client, f"/api/v1/communities/{community_id}/service-areas", json={ 78 "name": "Downtown", 79 "address_line1": "200 Congress Ave", 80 "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701", 81 }, headers=admin_h) 82 83 # Bind group leader 84 gl_resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/login", 85 json={"username": "gl_alice", "password": "AlicePass1234!"}) 86 gl_token = gl_resp.json.get("token", "") 87 # Get gl user_id by registering again to extract it 88 reg = _post(client, "/api/v1/auth/register", 89 json={"username": "gl_alice", "password": "AlicePass1234!", "role": "Group Leader"}) 90 gl_uid = reg.json.get("user_id") if reg.status_code == 201 else None 91 if gl_uid: 92 _post(client, f"/api/v1/communities/{community_id}/leader-binding", 93 json={"user_id": gl_uid}, headers=admin_h) 94 95 # Commission rule 96 _post(client, f"/api/v1/communities/{community_id}/commission-rules", json={ 97 "rate": 8.0, "floor": 2.0, "ceiling": 12.0, "settlement_cycle": "weekly", 98 }, headers=admin_h) 99 100 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 101 # 3. Products 102 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 103 products = [ 104 {"sku": "LAPTOP-001", "name": "ProBook 15", "brand": "TechCo", 105 "category": "Electronics", "price_usd": 999.99, 106 "tags": ["laptop", "pro"], "attributes": [{"key": "ram", "value": "16GB"}]}, 107 {"sku": "MOUSE-001", "name": "ErgoMouse", "brand": "TechCo", 108 "category": "Electronics", "price_usd": 49.99, "tags": ["mouse", "ergonomic"]}, 109 {"sku": "BOOK-001", "name": "Python Mastery", "brand": "PressHouse", 110 "category": "Books", "price_usd": 34.99, "tags": ["python", "programming"]}, 111 ] 112 product_ids = {} 113 for p in products: 114 resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/products", json=p, headers=admin_h) 115 if resp.status_code == 201: 116 product_ids[p["sku"]] = resp.json["product_id"] 117 print(f" product {p['sku']:20s} id={product_ids[p['sku']][:8]}…") 118 119 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 120 # 4. Warehouse + inventory 121 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 122 wh_resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/warehouses", 123 json={"name": "Austin Main", "location": "Austin, TX"}, 124 headers=admin_h) 125 if wh_resp.status_code == 201: 126 wh_id = wh_resp.json["warehouse_id"] 127 print(f" warehouse {wh_id[:8]}…") 128 129 bin_resp = _post(client, f"/api/v1/warehouses/{wh_id}/bins", 130 json={"bin_code": "A-01", "description": "Aisle 1"}, 131 headers=admin_h) 132 133 for sku, pid in product_ids.items(): 134 _post(client, "/api/v1/inventory/receipts", json={ 135 "sku_id": pid, "warehouse_id": wh_id, "quantity": 50, "unit_cost_usd": 10.0, 136 }, headers=admin_h) 137 print(f" receipt {sku}") 138 139 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 140 # 5. Content 141 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 142 art_resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/content", json={ 143 "type": "article", 144 "title": "Getting Started with Austin Community", 145 "body": "

Welcome to the Austin Community platform.

", 146 "tags": ["welcome", "getting-started"], 147 "categories": ["announcements"], 148 }, headers=admin_h) 149 if art_resp.status_code == 201: 150 cid = art_resp.json["content_id"] 151 _post(client, f"/api/v1/content/{cid}/publish", headers=admin_h) 152 print(f" content {cid[:8]}… published") 153 154 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 155 # 6. Template 156 # ---------------------------------------------------------------- 157 tmpl_resp = _post(client, "/api/v1/templates", json={ 158 "name": "Product Capture Form", 159 "fields": [ 160 {"name": "product_name", "type": "text", "required": True}, 161 {"name": "quantity", "type": "number", "required": True}, 162 {"name": "notes", "type": "textarea", "required": False}, 163 ], 164 }, headers=admin_h) 165 if tmpl_resp.status_code == 201: 166 tid = tmpl_resp.json["template_id"] 167 _post(client, f"/api/v1/templates/{tid}/publish", headers=admin_h) 168 print(f" template {tid[:8]}… published") 169 170 print("\nSeed complete.") 171 172 173 if __name__ == "__main__": 174 seed() 175 Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 1	"""Commission rules and settlement service."""
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta, date
3	
4	from app.extensions import db
5	from app.models.commission import CommissionRule, SettlementRun, SettlementDispute, SETTLEMENT_CYCLES
6	from app.models.user import User
7	from app.errors import NotFoundError, UnprocessableError, ForbiddenError, AppError
8	
9	_DISPUTE_WINDOW_DAYS = 2
10	_SYSTEM_DEFAULT_RATE = 6.0
11	
12	
13	class CommissionService:
14	
15	    # --- Commission Rules ---
16	
17	    @staticmethod
18	    def create_rule(community_id: str, data: dict) -> CommissionRule:
19	        rate = float(data.get("rate", _SYSTEM_DEFAULT_RATE))
20	        floor = float(data.get("floor", 0.0))
21	        ceiling = float(data.get("ceiling", 15.0))
22	        if not (0 <= floor <= rate <= ceiling <= 15.0):
23	            raise AppError("invalid_rate_range", "Must satisfy: 0 ≤ floor ≤ rate ≤ ceiling ≤ 15", status_code=400)
24	        settlement_cycle = data.get("settlement_cycle", "weekly")
25	        if settlement_cycle not in SETTLEMENT_CYCLES:
26	            raise AppError("invalid_cycle", "settlement_cycle must be 'weekly' or 'biweekly'",
27	                           field="settlement_cycle", status_code=400)
28	        rule = CommissionRule(
29	            community_id=community_id,
30	            product_category=data.get("product_category"),
31	            rate=rate,
32	            floor=floor,
33	            ceiling=ceiling,
34	            settlement_cycle=settlement_cycle,
35	        )
36	        db.session.add(rule)
37	        db.session.commit()
38	        return rule
39	
40	    @staticmethod
41	    def list_rules(community_id: str) -> list:
42	        rules = CommissionRule.query.filter_by(community_id=community_id, deleted_at=None).all()
43	        return [r.to_dict() for r in rules]
44	
45	    @staticmethod
46	    def resolve_rate(community_id: str, product_category: str | None = None) -> float:
47	        """
48	        Apply commission resolution precedence:
49	          1. Category-specific rule for this community + category
50	          2. Community default rule (product_category IS NULL)
51	          3. System default 6.0%
52	        """
53	        if product_category:
54	            rule = CommissionRule.query.filter_by(
55	                community_id=community_id,
56	                product_category=product_category,
57	                deleted_at=None,
58	            ).first()
59	            if rule:
60	                return rule.rate
61	
62	        default = CommissionRule.query.filter(
63	            CommissionRule.community_id == community_id,
64	            CommissionRule.product_category.is_(None),
65	            CommissionRule.deleted_at.is_(None),
66	        ).first()
67	        if default:
68	            return default.rate
69	
70	        return _SYSTEM_DEFAULT_RATE
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def assert_can_read(community_id: str, user: User) -> None:
74	        if user.role in ("Administrator", "Operations Manager"):
75	            return
76	        if user.role == "Group Leader":
77	            from app.models.community import GroupLeaderBinding
78	            binding = GroupLeaderBinding.query.filter_by(
79	                community_id=community_id, user_id=user.user_id, active=True
80	            ).first()
81	            if binding:
82	                return
83	        raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Access denied")
84	
85	    @staticmethod
86	    def update_rule(community_id: str, rule_id: str, data: dict) -> CommissionRule:
87	        rule = db.session.get(CommissionRule, rule_id)
88	        if rule is None or str(rule.community_id) != community_id or rule.deleted_at is not None:
89	            raise NotFoundError("commission_rule")
90	
91	        # Compute new values without touching the model yet (avoids dirty-session issues)
92	        new_rate = float(data["rate"]) if "rate" in data else rule.rate
93	        new_floor = float(data["floor"]) if "floor" in data else rule.floor
94	        new_ceiling = float(data["ceiling"]) if "ceiling" in data else rule.ceiling
95	        new_cycle = data.get("settlement_cycle", rule.settlement_cycle)
96	
97	        if new_cycle not in SETTLEMENT_CYCLES:
98	            raise AppError("invalid_cycle", "settlement_cycle must be 'weekly' or 'biweekly'",
99	                           field="settlement_cycle", status_code=400)
100	        if not (0 <= new_floor <= new_rate <= new_ceiling <= 15.0):
101	            raise AppError("invalid_rate_range", "Must satisfy: 0 ≤ floor ≤ rate ≤ ceiling ≤ 15", status_code=400)
102	
103	        rule.rate = new_rate
104	        rule.floor = new_floor
105	        rule.ceiling = new_ceiling
106	        rule.settlement_cycle = new_cycle
107	        db.session.commit()
108	        return rule
109	
110	    @staticmethod
111	    def delete_rule(community_id: str, rule_id: str) -> None:
112	        rule = db.session.get(CommissionRule, rule_id)
113	        if rule is None or str(rule.community_id) != community_id:
114	            raise NotFoundError("commission_rule")
115	        rule.deleted_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
116	        db.session.commit()
117	
118	    # --- Settlements ---
119	
120	    @staticmethod
121	    def create_settlement(data: dict, actor: User) -> tuple:
122	        """
123	        Returns (settlement, created: bool).
124	        If the idempotency_key already exists, returns (existing, False) so the
125	        route can respond 409 with the existing settlement object.
126	        """
127	        key = data.get("idempotency_key")
128	        if not key:
129	            raise AppError("idempotency_key_required", "idempotency_key is required",
130	                           field="idempotency_key", status_code=400)
131	        existing = SettlementRun.query.filter_by(idempotency_key=key).first()
132	        if existing:
133	            return existing, False  # idempotent duplicate
134	
135	        settlement = SettlementRun(
136	            community_id=data["community_id"],
137	            idempotency_key=key,
138	            status="pending",
139	            period_start=date.fromisoformat(data["period_start"]),
140	            period_end=date.fromisoformat(data["period_end"]),
141	        )
142	        db.session.add(settlement)
143	        db.session.commit()
144	        return settlement, True
145	
146	    @staticmethod
147	    def get_settlement(settlement_id: str) -> SettlementRun:
148	        s = db.session.get(SettlementRun, settlement_id)
149	        if s is None:
150	            raise NotFoundError("settlement")
151	        return s
152	
153	    @staticmethod
154	    def assert_can_read_settlement(settlement_id: str, user: User) -> None:
155	        if user.role in ("Administrator", "Operations Manager"):
156	            return
157	        if user.role == "Group Leader":
158	            s = CommissionService.get_settlement(settlement_id)
159	            from app.models.community import GroupLeaderBinding
160	            binding = GroupLeaderBinding.query.filter_by(
161	                community_id=s.community_id, user_id=user.user_id, active=True
162	            ).first()
163	            if binding:
164	                return
165	        raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Access denied")
166	
167	    @staticmethod
168	    def file_dispute(settlement_id: str, data: dict, actor: User) -> SettlementDispute:
169	        s = CommissionService.get_settlement(settlement_id)
170	        # Dispute window: 2 days from finalized_at (or created_at if not yet finalized)
171	        window_start = s.finalized_at or s.created_at
172	        deadline = window_start + timedelta(days=_DISPUTE_WINDOW_DAYS)
173	        if datetime.now(timezone.utc) > deadline.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc):
174	            raise UnprocessableError("dispute_window_expired", "Dispute window has closed")
175	        dispute = SettlementDispute(
176	            settlement_id=settlement_id,
177	            filed_by=actor.user_id,
178	            reason=data["reason"],
179	            disputed_amount=float(data.get("disputed_amount", 0)),
180	        )
181	        db.session.add(dispute)
182	        db.session.commit()
183	        return dispute
184	
185	    @staticmethod
186	    def resolve_dispute(settlement_id: str, dispute_id: str, data: dict) -> SettlementDispute:
187	        dispute = db.session.get(SettlementDispute, dispute_id)
188	        if dispute is None or str(dispute.settlement_id) != settlement_id:
189	            raise NotFoundError("dispute")
190	        resolution = data.get("resolution")
191	        if resolution not in ("resolved", "rejected"):
192	            raise AppError("invalid_resolution", "resolution must be 'resolved' or 'rejected'",
193	                           field="resolution", status_code=400)
194	        dispute.status = resolution
195	        dispute.resolution_notes = data.get("notes")
196	        dispute.resolved_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
197	        db.session.commit()
198	        return dispute
199	
200	    @staticmethod
201	    def finalize(settlement_id: str, actor: User) -> SettlementRun:
202	        s = CommissionService.get_settlement(settlement_id)
203	        open_disputes = SettlementDispute.query.filter_by(
204	            settlement_id=settlement_id, status="open"
205	        ).count()
206	        if open_disputes > 0:
207	            raise UnprocessableError(
208	                "settlement_blocked_by_open_dispute",
209	                "Cannot finalize while disputes are open",
210	            )
211	        s.status = "completed"
212	        s.finalized_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
213	        db.session.commit()
214	        return s
215

1	"""Admin ticket and reporting service."""
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone
3	
4	from app.extensions import db
5	from app.models.admin import AdminTicket
6	from app.models.user import User
7	from app.errors import NotFoundError, ForbiddenError
8	from app.services.audit_service import AuditService
9	from flask import g
10	
11	
12	def _cid() -> str:
13	    return getattr(g, "correlation_id", "n/a")
14	
15	
16	class AdminService:
17	
18	    @staticmethod
19	    def create_ticket(data: dict, actor: User) -> AdminTicket:
20	        ticket = AdminTicket(
21	            type=data["type"],
22	            subject=data["subject"],
23	            body=data["body"],
24	            target_type=data.get("target_type"),
25	            target_id=data.get("target_id"),
26	            created_by=actor.user_id,
27	        )
28	        db.session.add(ticket)
29	        db.session.commit()
30	        return ticket
31	
32	    @staticmethod
33	    def list_tickets(params: dict, requester: User) -> dict:
34	        q = AdminTicket.query
35	        if requester.role == "Moderator":
36	            q = q.filter(AdminTicket.created_by == requester.user_id)
37	        if params.get("status"):
38	            q = q.filter(AdminTicket.status == params["status"])
39	        if params.get("type"):
40	            q = q.filter(AdminTicket.type == params["type"])
41	        page = params.get("page", 1)
42	        page_size = params.get("page_size", 20)
43	        total = q.count()
44	        items = q.order_by(AdminTicket.created_at.desc()).offset((page - 1) * page_size).limit(page_size).all()
45	        return {"total": total, "page": page, "page_size": page_size, "items": [t.to_dict() for t in items]}
46	
47	    @staticmethod
48	    def update_ticket(ticket_id: str, data: dict, actor: User) -> AdminTicket:
49	        ticket = db.session.get(AdminTicket, ticket_id)
50	        if ticket is None:
51	            raise NotFoundError("admin_ticket")
52	        if "status" in data:
53	            ticket.status = data["status"]
54	            if data["status"] == "closed":
55	                ticket.resolved_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
56	        if "resolution_notes" in data:
57	            ticket.resolution_notes = data["resolution_notes"]
58	        AuditService.append(
59	            action_type="moderation", actor_id=actor.user_id,
60	            target_type="admin_ticket", target_id=str(ticket_id),
61	            after={"status": ticket.status},
62	            correlation_id=_cid(),
63	        )
64	        db.session.commit()
65	        return ticket
66	
67	    @staticmethod
68	    def group_leader_performance(params: dict, requester: User) -> dict:
69	        """
70	        Scaffold: returns structure. Full implementation queries SettlementRuns,
71	        InventoryTransactions, and Products to compute actual metrics.
72	        Row-level scoping: Group Leaders may only query their bound community.
73	        """
74	        community_id = params.get("community_id")
75	
76	        if requester.role == "Group Leader":
77	            from app.models.community import GroupLeaderBinding
78	            binding = GroupLeaderBinding.query.filter_by(
79	                user_id=requester.user_id, active=True,
80	            ).first()
81	            if binding is None or (community_id and str(binding.community_id) != community_id):
82	                raise ForbiddenError("forbidden", "Access restricted to your bound community")
83	            community_id = str(binding.community_id)
84	
85	        return {
86	            "community_id": community_id,
87	            "period": {"from": params.get("from"), "to": params.get("to")},
88	            "total_orders": 0,
89	            "total_order_value_usd": 0.0,
90	            "commission_earned_usd": 0.0,
91	            "top_products": [],
92	        }
93

1	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g
2	from app.middleware.auth import require_auth
3	from app.middleware.rbac import require_roles
4	from app.services.audit_service import AuditService
5	from app.services.admin_service import AdminService
6	
7	admin_bp = Blueprint("admin", __name__)
8	
9	_TICKET_ROLES = ("Administrator", "Operations Manager", "Moderator")
10	
11	
12	@admin_bp.get("/audit-log")
13	@require_auth
14	@require_roles("Administrator")
15	def get_audit_log():
16	    params = {
17	        "action_type": request.args.get("action_type"),
18	        "user_id": request.args.get("user_id"),
19	        "from": request.args.get("from"),
20	        "to": request.args.get("to"),
21	        "page": int(request.args.get("page", 1)),
22	        "page_size": min(int(request.args.get("page_size", 20)), 100),
23	    }
24	    return jsonify(AuditService.query(params))
25	
26	
27	@admin_bp.post("/admin/tickets")
28	@require_auth
29	@require_roles(*_TICKET_ROLES)
30	def create_ticket():
31	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
32	    ticket = AdminService.create_ticket(data, actor=g.current_user)
33	    return jsonify(ticket.to_dict()), 201
34	
35	
36	@admin_bp.get("/admin/tickets")
37	@require_auth
38	@require_roles(*_TICKET_ROLES)
39	def list_tickets():
40	    params = {
41	        "status": request.args.get("status"),
42	        "type": request.args.get("type"),
43	        "page": int(request.args.get("page", 1)),
44	        "page_size": min(int(request.args.get("page_size", 20)), 100),
45	    }
46	    return jsonify(AdminService.list_tickets(params, requester=g.current_user))
47	
48	
49	@admin_bp.patch("/admin/tickets/<ticket_id>")
50	@require_auth
51	@require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
52	def update_ticket(ticket_id):
53	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
54	    ticket = AdminService.update_ticket(ticket_id, data, actor=g.current_user)
55	    return jsonify(ticket.to_dict())
56	
57	
58	@admin_bp.get("/admin/reports/group-leader-performance")
59	@require_auth
60	def group_leader_performance():
61	    params = {
62	        "community_id": request.args.get("community_id"),
63	        "from": request.args.get("from"),
64	        "to": request.args.get("to"),
65	    }
66	    return jsonify(AdminService.group_leader_performance(params, requester=g.current_user))
67

1	"""Content item service — articles, books, chapters, attachments."""
2	import hashlib
3	import json
4	import os
5	from datetime import datetime, timezone
6	
7	import bleach
8	
9	from flask import current_app
10	from app.extensions import db
11	from app.models.content import ContentItem, ContentVersion, Attachment
12	from app.models.user import User
13	from app.models.audit import AuditLog
14	from app.errors import NotFoundError, AppError
15	from sqlalchemy import desc
16	from flask import g
17	
18	_ALLOWED_TAGS = [
19	    "p", "br", "strong", "em", "u", "h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6",
20	    "ul", "ol", "li", "blockquote", "code", "pre", "a", "img",
21	]
22	_ALLOWED_ATTRS = {"a": ["href"], "img": ["src", "alt"]}
23	
24	
25	def _sanitize(body: str) -> str:
26	    return bleach.clean(body, tags=_ALLOWED_TAGS, attributes=_ALLOWED_ATTRS, strip=True)
27	
28	
29	def _cid() -> str:
30	    return getattr(g, "correlation_id", "n/a")
31	
32	
33	class ContentService:
34	
35	    @staticmethod
36	    def _get_or_404(content_id: str) -> ContentItem:
37	        item = db.session.get(ContentItem, content_id)
38	        if item is None or item.deleted_at is not None:
39	            raise NotFoundError("content_item")
40	        return item
41	
42	    @staticmethod
43	    def create(data: dict, author: User) -> dict:
44	        item = ContentItem(
45	            type=data["type"],
46	            parent_id=data.get("parent_id"),
47	            title=data["title"],
48	            status="draft",
49	            created_by=author.user_id,
50	        )
51	        db.session.add(item)
52	        db.session.flush()
53	
54	        body = _sanitize(data.get("body", ""))
55	        version = ContentVersion(
56	            content_id=item.content_id,
57	            version=1,
58	            body=body,
59	            tags=json.dumps(data.get("tags", [])),
60	            categories=json.dumps(data.get("categories", [])),
61	            status="draft",
62	            created_by=author.user_id,
63	        )
64	        db.session.add(version)
65	        db.session.commit()
66	        result = item.to_dict()
67	        result.update(version.to_dict())
68	        return result
69	
70	    @staticmethod
71	    def get(content_id: str, version: int | None = None) -> dict:
72	        item = ContentService._get_or_404(content_id)
73	        if version:
74	            v = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=content_id, version=version).first()
75	        else:
76	            v = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=content_id, version=item.current_version).first()
77	        if v is None:
78	            raise NotFoundError("content_version")
79	        result = item.to_dict()
80	        result.update(v.to_dict())
81	        return result
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def update(content_id: str, data: dict, author: User) -> dict:
85	        item = ContentService._get_or_404(content_id)
86	        latest_v = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=content_id, version=item.current_version).first()
87	
88	        new_version_num = item.current_version + 1
89	        body = _sanitize(data.get("body", latest_v.body if latest_v else ""))
90	        new_v = ContentVersion(
91	            content_id=item.content_id,
92	            version=new_version_num,
93	            body=body,
94	            tags=json.dumps(data.get("tags", json.loads(latest_v.tags) if latest_v else [])),
95	            categories=json.dumps(data.get("categories", json.loads(latest_v.categories) if latest_v else [])),
96	            status="draft",
97	            created_by=author.user_id,
98	        )
99	        if "title" in data:
100	            item.title = data["title"]
101	        item.current_version = new_version_num
102	        db.session.add(new_v)
103	        db.session.commit()
104	        result = item.to_dict()
105	        result.update(new_v.to_dict())
106	        return result
107	
108	    @staticmethod
109	    def publish(content_id: str, actor: User) -> dict:
110	        item = ContentService._get_or_404(content_id)
111	        v = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=content_id, version=item.current_version).first()
112	        if v is None:
113	            raise NotFoundError("content_version")
114	        v.status = "published"
115	        v.published_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
116	        item.status = "published"
117	        db.session.add(AuditLog(
118	            action_type="content", actor_id=actor.user_id,
119	            target_type="content_item", target_id=str(content_id),
120	            after_state=json.dumps({"status": "published", "version": v.version}),
121	            correlation_id=_cid(),
122	        ))
123	        db.session.commit()
124	        result = item.to_dict()
125	        result.update(v.to_dict())
126	        return result
127	
128	    @staticmethod
129	    def rollback(content_id: str, target_version: int, actor: User) -> dict:
130	        item = ContentService._get_or_404(content_id)
131	        v = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=content_id, version=target_version).first()
132	        if v is None:
133	            raise NotFoundError("content_version")
134	        item.current_version = target_version
135	        item.status = v.status
136	        db.session.add(AuditLog(
137	            action_type="content", actor_id=actor.user_id,
138	            target_type="content_item", target_id=str(content_id),
139	            after_state=json.dumps({"rollback_to_version": target_version}),
140	            correlation_id=_cid(),
141	        ))
142	        db.session.commit()
143	        result = item.to_dict()
144	        result.update(v.to_dict())
145	        return result
146	
147	    @staticmethod
148	    def list_versions(content_id: str) -> list:
149	        ContentService._get_or_404(content_id)
150	        versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=content_id).order_by(ContentVersion.version.asc()).all()
151	        return [{"version": v.version, "status": v.status, "created_at": v.created_at.isoformat()} for v in versions]
152	
153	    @staticmethod
154	    def add_attachment(content_id: str, file, actor: User) -> Attachment:
155	        ContentService._get_or_404(content_id)
156	        if file is None:
157	            raise AppError("file_required", "No file provided", status_code=400)
158	
159	        max_bytes = current_app.config["ATTACHMENT_MAX_BYTES"]
160	        allowed_mime = current_app.config["ATTACHMENT_ALLOWED_MIME"]
161	        attach_dir = current_app.config["ATTACHMENT_DIR"]
162	
163	        data = file.read()
164	        if len(data) > max_bytes:
165	            raise AppError("file_too_large", "File exceeds 25 MB limit", status_code=413)
166	
167	        mime = file.mimetype or "application/octet-stream"
168	        if mime not in allowed_mime:
169	            raise AppError("unsupported_media_type", f"Allowed types: {', '.join(allowed_mime)}", status_code=415)
170	
171	        sha256 = hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest()
172	        filename = f"{sha256}_{file.filename}"
173	        local_path = os.path.join(attach_dir, filename)
174	        os.makedirs(attach_dir, exist_ok=True)
175	        with open(local_path, "wb") as f:
176	            f.write(data)
177	
178	        attachment = Attachment(
179	            content_id=content_id,
180	            filename=file.filename,
181	            mime_type=mime,
182	            size_bytes=len(data),
183	            sha256=sha256,
184	            local_path=local_path,
185	            created_by=actor.user_id,
186	        )
187	        db.session.add(attachment)
188	        db.session.commit()
189	        return attachment
190	
191	    @staticmethod
192	    def list_attachments(content_id: str) -> list:
193	        ContentService._get_or_404(content_id)
194	        attachments = Attachment.query.filter_by(content_id=content_id, deleted_at=None).all()
195	        return [a.to_dict() for a in attachments]
196	
197	    @staticmethod
198	    def delete_attachment(content_id: str, attachment_id: str) -> None:
199	        att = db.session.get(Attachment, attachment_id)
200	        if att is None or str(att.content_id) != content_id:
201	            raise NotFoundError("attachment")
202	        att.deleted_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
203	        db.session.commit()
204

1	"""
2	API functional tests for admin tickets, audit log, and health endpoints.
3	
4	Covers: ticket creation, listing, status filtering, closing, audit log access,
5	        RBAC enforcement for tickets and audit log, and health/readiness checks.
6	All tests use the Flask test client against /api/v1/admin/* and related endpoints.
7	"""
8	import uuid
9	import pytest
10	
11	BASE = "/api/v1"
12	
13	
14	def _create_ticket(client, headers, ticket_type="moderation", **overrides):
15	    payload = {
16	        "type": ticket_type,
17	        "subject": f"Issue {uuid.uuid4().hex[:4]}",
18	        "body": "Please review this matter.",
19	    }
20	    payload.update(overrides)
21	    return client.post(f"{BASE}/admin/tickets", json=payload, headers=headers)
22	
23	
24	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
25	# Admin tickets
26	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
27	
28	def test_create_ticket_201(client, auth_headers):
29	    """POST /admin/tickets returns 201 with ticket_id and status=open."""
30	    resp = _create_ticket(client, auth_headers)
31	    assert resp.status_code == 201
32	    data = resp.json
33	    assert "ticket_id" in data
34	    assert data["status"] == "open"
35	
36	
37	def test_create_ticket_member_403(client, member_headers):
38	    """Member token POST /admin/tickets returns 403."""
39	    resp = _create_ticket(client, member_headers)
40	    assert resp.status_code == 403
41	
42	
43	def test_list_tickets_200(client, auth_headers):
44	    """GET /admin/tickets returns 200 with paginated response."""
45	    _create_ticket(client, auth_headers)
46	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/admin/tickets", headers=auth_headers)
47	    assert resp.status_code == 200
48	    data = resp.json
49	    assert "total" in data or "items" in data or isinstance(data, list)
50	
51	
52	def test_list_tickets_status_filter(client, auth_headers):
53	    """GET /admin/tickets?status=open returns only open tickets."""
54	    _create_ticket(client, auth_headers)
55	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/admin/tickets?status=open", headers=auth_headers)
56	    assert resp.status_code == 200
57	    data = resp.json
58	    items = data.get("items", data) if isinstance(data, dict) else data
59	    assert isinstance(items, list)
60	    if len(items) > 0:
61	        assert all(t["status"] == "open" for t in items)
62	
63	
64	def test_close_ticket_200(client, auth_headers):
65	    """PATCH /admin/tickets/{id} with status=closed returns 200 with status=closed."""
66	    ticket = _create_ticket(client, auth_headers).json
67	    tid = ticket["ticket_id"]
68	    resp = client.patch(f"{BASE}/admin/tickets/{tid}", json={"status": "closed"}, headers=auth_headers)
69	    assert resp.status_code == 200
70	    assert resp.json["status"] == "closed"
71	
72	
73	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	# Audit log
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	def test_audit_log_admin_200(client, auth_headers):
78	    """GET /audit-log returns 200 with items array for admin."""
79	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/audit-log", headers=auth_headers)
80	    assert resp.status_code == 200
81	    data = resp.json
82	    assert "items" in data
83	
84	
85	def test_audit_log_member_403(client, member_headers):
86	    """Member token GET /audit-log returns 403."""
87	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/audit-log", headers=member_headers)
88	    assert resp.status_code == 403
89	
90	
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	# Health endpoints
93	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
94	
95	def test_health_liveness_200(client):
96	    """GET /health returns 200 with {status: ok, db: ok, version: string}."""
97	    resp = client.get("/health")
98	    assert resp.status_code == 200
99	    data = resp.json
100	    assert data["status"] == "ok"
101	    assert "db" in data
102	    assert "version" in data
103	    assert isinstance(data["version"], str)
104	
105	
106	def test_health_readiness(client):
107	    """GET /health/ready returns 200 or 503 — either is acceptable in test environment."""
108	    resp = client.get("/health/ready")
109	    assert resp.status_code in (200, 503)
110	    assert "status" in resp.json
111	
112	
113	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
114	# Group-leader performance report (REPORT-1)
115	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	
117	def _create_community(client, headers):
118	    payload = {
119	        "name": f"Comm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
120	        "address_line1": "1 Main St",
121	        "city": "Austin",
122	        "state": "TX",
123	        "zip": "78701",
124	    }
125	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/communities", json=payload, headers=headers)
126	    assert resp.status_code == 201
127	    return resp.json["community_id"]
128	
129	
130	def test_report_admin_no_filter_200(client, auth_headers):
131	    """Admin GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance (no filter) returns 200 with correct shape."""
132	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance", headers=auth_headers)
133	    assert resp.status_code == 200
134	    data = resp.json
135	    assert "period" in data
136	    assert "total_orders" in data
137	    assert "total_order_value_usd" in data
138	    assert "commission_earned_usd" in data
139	    assert "top_products" in data
140	    assert isinstance(data["top_products"], list)
141	
142	
143	def test_report_admin_with_community_id_200(client, auth_headers):
144	    """Admin GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id=X returns 200 with community_id echoed."""
145	    comm_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
146	    resp = client.get(
147	        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id={comm_id}",
148	        headers=auth_headers,
149	    )
150	    assert resp.status_code == 200
151	    data = resp.json
152	    assert data["community_id"] == comm_id
153	
154	
155	def test_report_admin_with_date_range_200(client, auth_headers):
156	    """Admin GET with from/to date params returns 200 with period echoed."""
157	    resp = client.get(
158	        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?from=2026-01-01&to=2026-03-31",
159	        headers=auth_headers,
160	    )
161	    assert resp.status_code == 200
162	    data = resp.json
163	    assert data["period"]["from"] == "2026-01-01"
164	    assert data["period"]["to"] == "2026-03-31"
165	
166	
167	def test_report_unauthenticated_401(client):
168	    """Unauthenticated GET /admin/reports/group-leader-performance returns 401."""
169	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance")
170	    assert resp.status_code == 401
171	
172	
173	def test_report_authenticated_member_200(client, member_headers):
174	    """Authenticated Member can call the report endpoint (no role restriction at route level)."""
175	    resp = client.get(
176	        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance",
177	        headers=member_headers,
178	    )
179	    assert resp.status_code == 200
180	    data = resp.json
181	    assert "total_orders" in data
182	
183	
184	def test_report_group_leader_own_community_200(client, auth_headers):
185	    """Group Leader can access the report for their bound community."""
186	    comm_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
187	
188	    # Register a Group Leader via service layer (public HTTP endpoint is Member-only)
189	    gl_name = f"gl_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
190	    with client.application.app_context():
191	        from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
192	        gl_user = AuthService.register(gl_name, "GlTestPass1!", role="Group Leader")
193	        gl_user_id = str(gl_user.user_id)
194	    gl_resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
195	        "username": gl_name, "password": "GlTestPass1!",
196	    })
197	    gl_token = gl_resp.json["token"]
198	    gl_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {gl_token}"}
199	
200	    # Bind GL to the community
201	    client.post(
202	        f"{BASE}/communities/{comm_id}/leader-binding",
203	        json={"user_id": gl_user_id},
204	        headers=auth_headers,
205	    )
206	
207	    resp = client.get(
208	        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id={comm_id}",
209	        headers=gl_headers,
210	    )
211	    assert resp.status_code == 200
212	    data = resp.json
213	    assert data["community_id"] == comm_id
214	
215	
216	def test_report_group_leader_other_community_403(client, auth_headers):
217	    """Group Leader cannot access report for a community they're not bound to."""
218	    comm1_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
219	    comm2_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
220	
221	    gl_name = f"gl_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
222	    with client.application.app_context():
223	        from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
224	        gl_user = AuthService.register(gl_name, "GlTestPass1!", role="Group Leader")
225	        gl_user_id = str(gl_user.user_id)
226	    gl_resp = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={
227	        "username": gl_name, "password": "GlTestPass1!",
228	    })
229	    gl_token = gl_resp.json["token"]
230	    gl_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {gl_token}"}
231	
232	    # Bind GL to comm1
233	    client.post(
234	        f"{BASE}/communities/{comm1_id}/leader-binding",
235	        json={"user_id": gl_user_id},
236	        headers=auth_headers,
237	    )
238	
239	    # Attempt to access comm2 — should be 403
240	    resp = client.get(
241	        f"{BASE}/admin/reports/group-leader-performance?community_id={comm2_id}",
242	        headers=gl_headers,
243	    )
244	    assert resp.status_code == 403
245

1	"""Messaging service (REST fallback; WebSocket handled in app/websocket.py)."""
2	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
3	
4	from app.extensions import db
5	from app.models.messaging import Message, MessageReceipt, MESSAGE_TYPES, RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER
6	from app.models.user import User
7	from app.errors import NotFoundError, AppError, ConflictError
8	
9	_OFFLINE_TTL_DAYS = 7
10	
11	
12	class MessagingService:
13	
14	    @staticmethod
15	    def send_message(data: dict, sender: User) -> Message:
16	        """Create a message and initial receipt (called from WebSocket and REST)."""
17	        msg_type = data.get("type", "text")
18	        if msg_type not in MESSAGE_TYPES:
19	            raise AppError("invalid_message_type",
20	                           f"type must be one of: {', '.join(MESSAGE_TYPES)}",
21	                           field="type", status_code=400)
22	
23	        recipient_id = data.get("recipient_id")
24	        group_id = data.get("group_id")
25	        if recipient_id and group_id:
26	            raise AppError("ambiguous_target",
27	                           "Specify recipient_id or group_id, not both",
28	                           status_code=400)
29	
30	        expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=_OFFLINE_TTL_DAYS)
31	        msg = Message(
32	            type=msg_type,
33	            sender_id=sender.user_id,
34	            recipient_id=recipient_id,
35	            group_id=group_id,
36	            body=data.get("body"),
37	            file_metadata=data.get("file_metadata"),
38	            expires_at=expires_at,
39	            correlation_id=data.get("correlation_id", ""),
40	        )
41	        db.session.add(msg)
42	        db.session.flush()
43	
44	        # Create delivery receipts: one for direct; one per member for group
45	        if recipient_id:
46	            db.session.add(MessageReceipt(
47	                message_id=msg.message_id,
48	                recipient_id=recipient_id,
49	                status="sent",
50	            ))
51	        elif group_id:
52	            from app.services.community_service import CommunityService
53	            member_ids = CommunityService.get_active_member_ids(group_id)
54	            for mid in member_ids:
55	                if mid != str(sender.user_id):
56	                    db.session.add(MessageReceipt(
57	                        message_id=msg.message_id,
58	                        recipient_id=mid,
59	                        status="sent",
60	                    ))
61	
62	        db.session.commit()
63	        return msg
64	
65	    @staticmethod
66	    def get_queued(user: User) -> list:
67	        """Return undelivered messages for this user (status='sent', not expired)."""
68	        receipts = (
69	            MessageReceipt.query
70	            .filter_by(recipient_id=user.user_id, status="sent")
71	            .join(Message)
72	            .filter(Message.expires_at > datetime.now(timezone.utc))
73	            .all()
74	        )
75	        return [r.message.to_dict() for r in receipts]
76	
77	    @staticmethod
78	    def update_receipt(message_id: str, status: str, user: User) -> dict:
79	        """Advance a receipt status (sent → delivered → read, never backward)."""
80	        if status not in ("delivered", "read"):
81	            raise AppError("invalid_status", "status must be 'delivered' or 'read'",
82	                           field="status", status_code=400)
83	        receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(
84	            message_id=message_id, recipient_id=user.user_id
85	        ).first()
86	        if receipt is None:
87	            raise NotFoundError("message_receipt")
88	
89	        current_order = RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER.get(receipt.status, 0)
90	        new_order = RECEIPT_STATUS_ORDER.get(status, 0)
91	        if new_order <= current_order:
92	            raise ConflictError(
93	                "status_regression",
94	                f"Cannot transition from '{receipt.status}' to '{status}'",
95	            )
96	
97	        receipt.status = status
98	        receipt.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
99	        db.session.commit()
100	        msg = receipt.message.to_dict()
101	        msg["delivery_status"] = status
102	        return msg
103

1	"""
2	WebSocket / STOMP messaging endpoints.
3	Flask-SocketIO is used as the STOMP-compatible transport.
4	Authentication uses the Bearer token from the STOMP CONNECT frame headers.
5	"""
6	import hashlib
7	from datetime import datetime, timezone
8	
9	from flask_socketio import emit, join_room, disconnect
10	from flask import request
11	
12	from app.extensions import socketio, db
13	from app.services.messaging_service import MessagingService
14	
15	
16	# sid → (user, token_hash) mapping; populated on connect, cleaned up on disconnect
17	_sid_user_map: dict = {}
18	
19	
20	@socketio.on("connect", namespace="/ws/messaging")
21	def on_connect(auth):
22	    token = (auth or {}).get("token", "")
23	    user = _load_user(token)
24	    if user is None:
25	        disconnect()
26	        return
27	    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
28	    _sid_user_map[request.sid] = (user, token_hash)
29	    # Join personal room for direct message delivery
30	    join_room(f"user_{user.user_id}")
31	    # Join community rooms for group message delivery
32	    from app.models.community import CommunityMember
33	    memberships = CommunityMember.query.filter_by(
34	        user_id=user.user_id, left_at=None
35	    ).all()
36	    for m in memberships:
37	        join_room(f"community_{m.community_id}")
38	
39	
40	@socketio.on("disconnect", namespace="/ws/messaging")
41	def on_disconnect():
42	    _sid_user_map.pop(request.sid, None)
43	
44	
45	def _get_authenticated_user():
46	    """Return the user for the current sid, re-validating the session token."""
47	    entry = _sid_user_map.get(request.sid)
48	    if entry is None:
49	        return None
50	    user, token_hash = entry
51	    from app.models.user import Session
52	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
53	    if session is None:
54	        return None
55	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
56	        _sid_user_map.pop(request.sid, None)
57	        disconnect()
58	        return None
59	    return user
60	
61	
62	@socketio.on("direct", namespace="/ws/messaging")
63	def on_direct(data):
64	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
65	    if user is None:
66	        return
67	    data["correlation_id"] = getattr(__import__("flask").g, "correlation_id", "")
68	    msg = MessagingService.send_message(data, sender=user)
69	    emit("message", msg.to_dict(), room=f"user_{data.get('recipient_id')}")
70	
71	
72	@socketio.on("group", namespace="/ws/messaging")
73	def on_group(data):
74	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
75	    if user is None:
76	        return
77	    msg = MessagingService.send_message(data, sender=user)
78	    emit("message", msg.to_dict(), room=f"community_{data.get('group_id')}")
79	
80	
81	@socketio.on("receipt", namespace="/ws/messaging")
82	def on_receipt(data):
83	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
84	    if user is None:
85	        return
86	    MessagingService.update_receipt(data["message_id"], data["status"], user=user)
87	
88	
89	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
90	# Helpers
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	
93	def _load_user(token: str):
94	    """Load user from raw token string without touching the HTTP request object."""
95	    if not token:
96	        return None
97	    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
98	    from app.models.user import Session, User
99	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
100	    if session is None:
101	        return None
102	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
103	        return None
104	    user = db.session.get(User, session.user_id)
105	    if user is None or user.deleted_at is not None:
106	        return None
107	    return user
108

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# ============================================================
3	# run_tests.sh — One-click test runner for the acceptance suite (required entry point).
4	# Run from repo/:  bash run_tests.sh
5	#
6	# - If this Python has pytest (after pip install -r requirements.txt), tests run on the host.
7	# - If pytest is missing (typical CI with only Docker), this script re-invokes itself inside
8	#   the app image so the same file path still satisfies "run run_tests.sh".
9	# ============================================================
10	set -euo pipefail
11	
12	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
13	REPO="$ROOT"
14	
15	# Re-run inside Docker once when host has no pytest (avoids requiring a second script name).
16	if [ "${RUN_TESTS_SH_IN_CONTAINER:-}" != "1" ]; then
17	  if ! python -m pytest --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
18	    if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker compose version >/dev/null 2>&1; then
19	      echo "[INFO]  pytest not found on host — running the same run_tests.sh inside Docker..."
20	      cd "$REPO"
21	      exec env RUN_TESTS_SH_IN_CONTAINER=1 \
22	        docker compose run -T --rm --no-deps \
23	        --entrypoint "" \
24	        -e RUN_TESTS_SH_IN_CONTAINER=1 \
25	        app bash /app/run_tests.sh "$@"
26	    fi
27	  fi
28	fi
29	
30	# ── Colour helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────
31	GREEN='\033[0;32m'; RED='\033[0;31m'; CYAN='\033[0;36m'; NC='\033[0m'
32	info()  { echo -e "${CYAN}[INFO]${NC}  $*"; }
33	ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC}  $*"; }
34	fail()  { echo -e "${RED}[FAIL]${NC}  $*"; }
35	
36	echo ""
37	echo "========================================================"
38	echo "  Acceptance Test Suite — Neighborhood Commerce System"
39	echo "========================================================"
40	echo ""
41	
42	# ── Prerequisites ────────────────────────────────────────────
43	info "Checking Python …"
44	python --version 2>&1 || { fail "python not found — install Python 3.12+"; exit 1; }
45	
46	info "Checking pytest …"
47	python -m pytest --version 2>&1 || {
48	  fail "pytest not found — run: pip install -r requirements.txt, or install Docker and docker compose"
49	  exit 1
50	}
51	
52	# ── Data directories and Fernet key ─────────────────────────
53	info "Ensuring data directories exist …"
54	mkdir -p "$REPO/data/keys" "$REPO/data/logs" "$REPO/data/attachments"
55	
56	if [ ! -f "$REPO/data/keys/secret.key" ]; then
57	    info "Generating Fernet encryption key …"
58	    python -c "
59	from cryptography.fernet import Fernet
60	with open('$REPO/data/keys/secret.key', 'wb') as f:
61	    f.write(Fernet.generate_key())
62	"
63	    ok "Key written to $REPO/data/keys/secret.key"
64	fi
65	
66	# ── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────────
67	export PYTHONPATH="$REPO"
68	export FERNET_KEY_PATH="$REPO/data/keys/secret.key"
69	export LOG_FILE="$REPO/data/logs/app.jsonl"
70	export ATTACHMENT_DIR="$REPO/data/attachments"
71	
72	cd "$ROOT"
73	
74	# ── Run unit tests ───────────────────────────────────────────
75	echo ""
76	echo "--------------------------------------------------------"
77	echo "  PHASE 1 — Unit Tests  (unit_tests/)"
78	echo "--------------------------------------------------------"
79	UNIT_RESULT=0
80	python -m pytest unit_tests/ \
81	    -v \
82	    --tb=short \
83	    --no-header \
84	    -q \
85	    2>&1 || UNIT_RESULT=$?
86	
87	# ── Run API functional tests ─────────────────────────────────
88	echo ""
89	echo "--------------------------------------------------------"
90	echo "  PHASE 2 — API Functional Tests  (API_tests/)"
91	echo "--------------------------------------------------------"
92	API_RESULT=0
93	python -m pytest API_tests/ \
94	    -v \
95	    --tb=short \
96	    --no-header \
97	    -q \
98	    2>&1 || API_RESULT=$?
99	
100	# ── Aggregate summary ────────────────────────────────────────
101	echo ""
102	echo "========================================================"
103	echo "  SUMMARY"
104	echo "========================================================"
105	python -m pytest unit_tests/ API_tests/ \
106	    --tb=no \
107	    -q \
108	    2>&1 | tail -5
109	
110	echo ""
111	if [ "$UNIT_RESULT" -eq 0 ] && [ "$API_RESULT" -eq 0 ]; then
112	    ok "All tests passed."
113	    exit 0
114	else
115	    fail "One or more tests failed."
116	    [ "$UNIT_RESULT"  -ne 0 ] && fail "  Unit tests:         FAILED (exit $UNIT_RESULT)"
117	    [ "$API_RESULT"   -ne 0 ] && fail "  API tests:          FAILED (exit $API_RESULT)"
118	    exit 1
119	fi
120
