89	class InventoryService:
90	
91	    # --- Warehouse & Bin ---
92	
93	    @staticmethod
94	    def create_warehouse(data: dict) -> Warehouse:
95	        cid = data.get("community_id")
96	        if cid:
97	            from app.services.community_service import CommunityService
98	            CommunityService._get_or_404(cid)
99	        wh = Warehouse(
100	            name=data["name"],
101	            location=data["location"],
102	            notes=data.get("notes"),
103	            community_id=cid or None,
104	        )
105	        db.session.add(wh)
106	        db.session.commit()
107	        return wh
108	
109	    @staticmethod
110	    def list_warehouses() -> list:
111	        return [w.to_dict() for w in Warehouse.query.all()]
112	
113	    @staticmethod
114	    def create_bin(warehouse_id: str, data: dict) -> Bin:
115	        wh = db.session.get(Warehouse, warehouse_id)
116	        if wh is None:
117	            raise NotFoundError("warehouse")
118	        b = Bin(warehouse_id=warehouse_id, bin_code=data["bin_code"], description=data.get("description"))
119	        db.session.add(b)
120	        db.session.commit()
121	        return b
122	
123	    @staticmethod
124	    def list_bins(warehouse_id: str) -> list:
125	        return [b.to_dict() for b in Bin.query.filter_by(warehouse_id=warehouse_id).all()]
126	
127	    # --- Inventory movements ---
128	
129	    @staticmethod
130	    def _get_or_create_lot(sku_id, warehouse_id, bin_id, lot_number, costing_method) -> InventoryLot:
131	        lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(
132	            sku_id=sku_id, warehouse_id=warehouse_id, bin_id=bin_id, lot_number=lot_number
133	        ).first()
134	        if lot is None:
135	            from app.models.catalog import Product
136	
137	            prod = db.session.get(Product, sku_id)
138	            threshold = int(prod.safety_stock_threshold or 0) if prod else 0
139	            lot = InventoryLot(
140	                sku_id=sku_id, warehouse_id=warehouse_id, bin_id=bin_id,
141	                lot_number=lot_number, costing_method=costing_method,
142	                safety_stock_threshold=threshold,
143	            )
144	            db.session.add(lot)
145	            db.session.flush()
146	        elif costing_method and lot.costing_method != costing_method:
147	            # Costing method is immutable after the first transaction (also enforced by DB trigger)
148	            raise UnprocessableError("costing_method_locked",
149	                                     "Costing method cannot be changed after first transaction")
150	        return lot
151	
152	    @staticmethod
153	    def record_receipt(data: dict, actor: User) -> InventoryTransaction:
154	        lot = InventoryService._get_or_create_lot(
155	            data["sku_id"], data["warehouse_id"], data.get("bin_id"),
156	            data.get("lot_number"), data.get("costing_method", "fifo"),
157	        )
158	        qty = int(data["quantity"])
159	        lot.on_hand_qty += qty
160	        if data.get("barcode"):
161	            lot.barcode = str(data["barcode"])[:128]
162	        if data.get("rfid"):
163	            lot.rfid = str(data["rfid"])[:128]
164	        serials = _serialize_serial_numbers(data.get("serial_numbers"))
165	        if serials is not None:
166	            lot.serial_numbers = serials
167	        occurred_at = (datetime.fromisoformat(data["occurred_at"])
168	                       if "occurred_at" in data else datetime.now(timezone.utc))
169	
170	        txn = InventoryTransaction(
171	            type="receipt", sku_id=lot.sku_id, warehouse_id=lot.warehouse_id,
172	            bin_id=lot.bin_id, lot_id=lot.lot_id, quantity_delta=qty,
173	            actor_id=actor.user_id, occurred_at=occurred_at,
174	            correlation_id=_cid(), reference=data.get("notes"),
175	            barcode=(str(data["barcode"])[:128] if data.get("barcode") else None),
176	            rfid=(str(data["rfid"])[:128] if data.get("rfid") else None),
177	            serial_numbers=serials,
178	        )
179	        db.session.add(txn)
180	
181	        unit_cost = float(data.get("unit_cost_usd", 0))
182	        if lot.costing_method == "fifo":
183	            db.session.add(CostLayer(
184	                sku_id=lot.sku_id, warehouse_id=lot.warehouse_id,
185	                quantity_remaining=qty, unit_cost_usd=unit_cost, received_at=occurred_at,
186	            ))
187	        else:  # moving_average
188	            _update_avg_cost(lot.sku_id, lot.warehouse_id, qty, unit_cost)
189	
190	        db.session.commit()
191	        return txn
192	
193	    @staticmethod
194	    def record_issue(data: dict, actor: User) -> InventoryTransaction:
195	        lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(
196	            sku_id=data["sku_id"], warehouse_id=data["warehouse_id"],
197	            bin_id=data.get("bin_id"), lot_number=data.get("lot_number"),
198	        ).first()
199	        if lot is None:
200	            raise NotFoundError("inventory_lot")
201	        qty = int(data["quantity"])
202	        if lot.on_hand_qty < qty:
203	            raise UnprocessableError("insufficient_stock", "Insufficient on-hand quantity")
204	
205	        lot.on_hand_qty -= qty
206	        # Issue resets slow-moving timer and clears the slow-moving flag
207	        lot.last_issue_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
208	        lot.slow_moving = False
209	
210	        occurred_at = (datetime.fromisoformat(data["occurred_at"])
211	                       if "occurred_at" in data else datetime.now(timezone.utc))
212	
213	        serials = _serialize_serial_numbers(data.get("serial_numbers"))
214	        txn = InventoryTransaction(
215	            type="issue", sku_id=lot.sku_id, warehouse_id=lot.warehouse_id,
216	            bin_id=lot.bin_id, lot_id=lot.lot_id, quantity_delta=-qty,
217	            reference=data.get("reference"), actor_id=actor.user_id,
218	            occurred_at=occurred_at, correlation_id=_cid(),
219	            barcode=(str(data["barcode"])[:128] if data.get("barcode") else lot.barcode),
220	            rfid=(str(data["rfid"])[:128] if data.get("rfid") else lot.rfid),
221	            serial_numbers=serials if serials is not None else lot.serial_numbers,
222	        )
223	        db.session.add(txn)
224	
225	        # Consume FIFO cost layers oldest-first; decrement avg-cost snapshot for MA lots
226	        if lot.costing_method == "fifo":
227	            _consume_fifo_layers(lot.sku_id, lot.warehouse_id, qty)
228	        else:
229	            _decrement_avg_cost(lot.sku_id, lot.warehouse_id, qty)
230	
231	        db.session.commit()
232	        return txn
233	
234	    @staticmethod
235	    def record_transfer(data: dict, actor: User) -> list:
236	        # 1. Look up source lot (raises if not found)
237	        source_lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(
238	            sku_id=data["sku_id"],
239	            warehouse_id=data["from_warehouse_id"],
240	            bin_id=data.get("from_bin_id"),
241	        ).first()
242	        if source_lot is None:
243	            raise NotFoundError("inventory_lot")
244	
245	        # 2. Capture cost and costing method before any mutation
246	        inherited_method = source_lot.costing_method
247	        transfer_cost = _current_cost_usd(data["sku_id"], data["from_warehouse_id"], inherited_method)
248	
249	        qty = int(data["quantity"])
250	        occurred_at = (datetime.fromisoformat(data["occurred_at"])
251	                       if "occurred_at" in data else datetime.now(timezone.utc))
252	
253	        # 3. Issue-side mutations (no commit)
254	        if source_lot.on_hand_qty < qty:
255	            raise UnprocessableError("insufficient_stock", "Insufficient on-hand quantity")
256	        source_lot.on_hand_qty -= qty
257	        source_lot.last_issue_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
258	        source_lot.slow_moving = False
259	
260	        issue_txn = InventoryTransaction(
261	            type="issue", sku_id=source_lot.sku_id, warehouse_id=source_lot.warehouse_id,
262	            bin_id=source_lot.bin_id, lot_id=source_lot.lot_id, quantity_delta=-qty,
263	            reference=data.get("reference"), actor_id=actor.user_id,
264	            occurred_at=occurred_at, correlation_id=_cid(),
265	        )
266	        db.session.add(issue_txn)
267	
268	        if inherited_method == "fifo":
269	            _consume_fifo_layers(source_lot.sku_id, source_lot.warehouse_id, qty)
270	        else:
271	            _decrement_avg_cost(source_lot.sku_id, source_lot.warehouse_id, qty)
272	
273	        # 4. Receipt-side mutations (no commit)
274	        dest_lot = InventoryService._get_or_create_lot(
275	            data["sku_id"], data["to_warehouse_id"], data.get("to_bin_id"),
276	            data.get("lot_number"), inherited_method,
277	        )
278	        dest_lot.on_hand_qty += qty
279	
280	        receipt_txn = InventoryTransaction(
281	            type="receipt", sku_id=dest_lot.sku_id, warehouse_id=dest_lot.warehouse_id,
282	            bin_id=dest_lot.bin_id, lot_id=dest_lot.lot_id, quantity_delta=qty,
283	            actor_id=actor.user_id, occurred_at=occurred_at,
284	            correlation_id=_cid(), reference=data.get("notes"),
285	        )
286	        db.session.add(receipt_txn)
287	
288	        if inherited_method == "fifo":
289	            db.session.add(CostLayer(
290	                sku_id=dest_lot.sku_id, warehouse_id=dest_lot.warehouse_id,
291	                quantity_remaining=qty, unit_cost_usd=transfer_cost, received_at=occurred_at,
292	            ))
293	        else:
294	            _update_avg_cost(dest_lot.sku_id, dest_lot.warehouse_id, qty, transfer_cost)
295	
296	        # 5. Single atomic commit
297	        db.session.commit()
298	        return [issue_txn, receipt_txn]
299	
300	    @staticmethod
301	    def record_adjustment(data: dict, actor: User) -> dict:
302	        if not data.get("reason"):
303	            raise AppError("reason_required", "reason is required for adjustments",
304	                           field="reason", status_code=400)
305	        lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(
306	            sku_id=data["sku_id"], warehouse_id=data["warehouse_id"], bin_id=data.get("bin_id")
307	        ).first()
308	        if lot is None:
309	            raise NotFoundError("inventory_lot")
310	        delta = int(data["quantity_delta"])
311	        before_qty = lot.on_hand_qty
312	        lot.on_hand_qty += delta
313	        occurred_at = (datetime.fromisoformat(data["occurred_at"])
314	                       if "occurred_at" in data else datetime.now(timezone.utc))
315	
316	        txn = InventoryTransaction(
317	            type="adjustment", sku_id=lot.sku_id, warehouse_id=lot.warehouse_id,
318	            bin_id=lot.bin_id, lot_id=lot.lot_id, quantity_delta=delta,
319	            reason=data["reason"], actor_id=actor.user_id,
320	            occurred_at=occurred_at, correlation_id=_cid(),
321	        )
322	        db.session.add(txn)
323	
324	        audit = AuditLog(
325	            action_type="inventory", actor_id=actor.user_id,
326	            target_type="inventory_lot", target_id=str(lot.lot_id),
327	            before_state=f'{{"qty": {before_qty}}}',
328	            after_state=f'{{"qty": {lot.on_hand_qty}}}',
329	            correlation_id=_cid(),
330	        )
331	        db.session.add(audit)
332	        db.session.commit()
333	        return {"transaction": txn.to_dict(), "audit_log_id": str(audit.log_id)}
334	
335	    @staticmethod
336	    def record_cycle_count(data: dict, actor: User) -> dict:
337	        cc = CycleCount(
338	            warehouse_id=data["warehouse_id"],
339	            actor_id=actor.user_id,
340	            counted_at=datetime.fromisoformat(data["counted_at"]),
341	        )
342	        db.session.add(cc)
343	        db.session.flush()
344	
345	        lines = []
346	        for line_data in data.get("lines", []):
347	            lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(
348	                sku_id=line_data["sku_id"], warehouse_id=data["warehouse_id"],
349	                bin_id=line_data.get("bin_id"),
350	            ).first()
351	            system_qty = lot.on_hand_qty if lot else 0
352	            counted_qty = int(line_data["counted_qty"])
353	            variance = counted_qty - system_qty
354	            if variance != 0 and not line_data.get("variance_reason"):
355	                raise AppError("variance_reason_required",
356	                               "variance_reason is required when variance != 0", status_code=400)
357	            ccl = CycleCountLine(
358	                cycle_count_id=cc.cycle_count_id,
359	                sku_id=line_data["sku_id"],
360	                bin_id=line_data.get("bin_id"),
361	                system_qty=system_qty,
362	                counted_qty=counted_qty,
363	                variance=variance,
364	                variance_reason=line_data.get("variance_reason"),
365	            )
366	            db.session.add(ccl)
367	            lines.append(ccl)
368	
369	        db.session.commit()
370	        return {"cycle_count_id": str(cc.cycle_count_id), "lines": [line.to_dict() for line in lines]}
371	
372	    @staticmethod
373	    def query_stock(params: dict) -> dict:
374	        q = InventoryLot.query
375	        if params.get("sku_id"):
376	            q = q.filter(InventoryLot.sku_id == params["sku_id"])
377	        if params.get("warehouse_id"):
378	            q = q.filter(InventoryLot.warehouse_id == params["warehouse_id"])
379	        if params.get("bin_id"):
380	            q = q.filter(InventoryLot.bin_id == params["bin_id"])
381	        if params.get("below_safety_stock"):
382	            q = q.filter(InventoryLot.on_hand_qty < InventoryLot.safety_stock_threshold)
383	        if params.get("slow_moving"):
384	            q = q.filter(InventoryLot.slow_moving.is_(True))
385	
386	        lots = q.all()
387	        items = []
388	        for lot in lots:
389	            d = lot.to_dict()
390	            d["below_safety_stock"] = lot.on_hand_qty < lot.safety_stock_threshold
391	            d["current_cost_usd"] = _current_cost_usd(lot.sku_id, lot.warehouse_id, lot.costing_method)
392	            items.append(d)
393	        return {"items": items}
394	
395	    @staticmethod
396	    def list_transactions(params: dict) -> dict:
397	        q = InventoryTransaction.query
398	        if params.get("sku_id"):
399	            q = q.filter(InventoryTransaction.sku_id == params["sku_id"])
400	        if params.get("warehouse_id"):
401	            q = q.filter(InventoryTransaction.warehouse_id == params["warehouse_id"])
402	        if params.get("type"):
403	            q = q.filter(InventoryTransaction.type == params["type"])
404	        if params.get("from"):
405	            q = q.filter(InventoryTransaction.occurred_at >= datetime.fromisoformat(params["from"]))
406	        if params.get("to"):
407	            q = q.filter(InventoryTransaction.occurred_at <= datetime.fromisoformat(params["to"]))
408	        page = params.get("page", 1)
409	        page_size = params.get("page_size", 20)
410	        total = q.count()
411	        items = (q.order_by(InventoryTransaction.occurred_at.desc())
412	                 .offset((page - 1) * page_size).limit(page_size).all())
413	        return {"total": total, "page": page, "page_size": page_size,
414	                "items": [t.to_dict() for t in items]}
415	
416	
417	def _update_avg_cost(sku_id, warehouse_id, added_qty: int, unit_cost: float) -> None:
418	    snap = AvgCostSnapshot.query.filter_by(sku_id=sku_id, warehouse_id=warehouse_id).first()
419	    if snap is None:
420	        snap = AvgCostSnapshot(sku_id=sku_id, warehouse_id=warehouse_id,
421	                               avg_cost_usd=unit_cost, on_hand_qty=added_qty)
422	        db.session.add(snap)
423	    else:
424	        total_value = snap.avg_cost_usd * snap.on_hand_qty + unit_cost * added_qty
425	        snap.on_hand_qty += added_qty
426	        snap.avg_cost_usd = total_value / snap.on_hand_qty if snap.on_hand_qty else 0
427	        snap.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
428	
429	
430	def _decrement_avg_cost(sku_id, warehouse_id, issued_qty: int) -> None:
431	    """Decrement AvgCostSnapshot.on_hand_qty when issuing from a moving-average lot."""
432	    snap = AvgCostSnapshot.query.filter_by(sku_id=sku_id, warehouse_id=warehouse_id).first()
433	    if snap is not None:
434	        snap.on_hand_qty = max(0, snap.on_hand_qty - issued_qty)
435	        snap.updated_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

9	class AuditLog(db.Model):
10	    """
11	    Append-only audit log.
12	    DB triggers (migration 0001) prevent UPDATE and DELETE on this table.
13	    Sensitive fields are redacted in before_state / after_state before insertion.
14	    """
15	    __tablename__ = "audit_log"
16	    __table_args__ = (
17	        sa.CheckConstraint(
18	            "action_type IN ('settlement', 'moderation', 'inventory', 'auth', 'content', 'template')",
19	            name="ck_audit_action_type",
20	        ),
21	    )
22	
23	    log_id = db.Column(GUID, primary_key=True, default=new_uuid)
24	    action_type = db.Column(db.String(32), nullable=False, index=True)
25	    # Nullable: system-initiated events (e.g. job-triggered) may have no actor
26	    actor_id = db.Column(GUID, db.ForeignKey("users.user_id"), nullable=True)
27	    target_type = db.Column(db.String(64), nullable=False)
28	    target_id = db.Column(db.String(36), nullable=False)
29	    before_state = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)  # JSON snapshot, sensitive fields redacted
30	    after_state = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)   # JSON snapshot
31	    occurred_at = db.Column(
32	        db.DateTime, nullable=False, default=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc), index=True
33	    )

35	
36	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
37	        import json

44	            "before": json.loads(self.before_state) if self.before_state else None,
45	            "after": json.loads(self.after_state) if self.after_state else None,
46	            "occurred_at": self.occurred_at.isoformat(),

1	import os
2	from flask import Flask
3	from sqlalchemy import event
4	from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
5	import sqlite3
6	
7	from .config import config_map, Config
8	from .extensions import db, migrate, socketio, scheduler
9	from .errors import register_error_handlers
10	from .middleware import register_middleware
11	from .crypto import init_fernet
12	
13	
14	def create_app(config_name: str | None = None) -> Flask:
15	    if config_name is None:
16	        config_name = os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "development")
17	
18	    app = Flask(__name__, instance_relative_config=False)
19	    app.config.from_object(config_map.get(config_name, Config))
20	
21	    # --- Fernet encryption key ---
22	    init_fernet(app.config["FERNET_KEY_PATH"])
23	
24	    # --- WebSocket event handlers ---
25	    # Import BEFORE socketio.init_app so @socketio.on decorators queue handlers
26	    # in socketio.handlers (server is still None).  Every subsequent init_app
27	    # call then re-registers those handlers on the new server, which is required
28	    # when create_app is called more than once in a test session.
29	    from . import websocket  # noqa: F401
30	
31	    # --- Extensions ---
32	    db.init_app(app)
33	    migrate.init_app(app, db)
34	    async_mode = app.config.get("SOCKETIO_ASYNC_MODE", "threading")
35	    socketio.init_app(app, async_mode=async_mode)
36	
37	    # Enable WAL mode for SQLite
38	    @event.listens_for(Engine, "connect")
39	    def set_sqlite_pragma(dbapi_conn, connection_record):
40	        if isinstance(dbapi_conn, sqlite3.Connection):
41	            cursor = dbapi_conn.cursor()
42	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
43	            cursor.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
44	            cursor.close()
45	
46	    # --- Middleware ---
47	    register_middleware(app)
48	
49	    # --- Blueprints ---
50	    _register_blueprints(app)
51	
52	    # --- Error handlers ---
53	    register_error_handlers(app)
54	
55	    # --- Background jobs ---
56	    if app.config.get("JOBS_ENABLED"):
57	        with app.app_context():
58	            _start_scheduler(app)
59	
60	    return app
61	
62	
63	def _register_blueprints(app: Flask) -> None:
64	    from .routes.health import health_bp
65	    from .routes.auth import auth_bp
66	    from .routes.users import users_bp
67	    from .routes.communities import communities_bp
68	    from .routes.commission import commission_bp
69	    from .routes.catalog import catalog_bp
70	    from .routes.search import search_bp
71	    from .routes.inventory import inventory_bp

73	    from .routes.content import content_bp
74	    from .routes.templates import templates_bp
75	    from .routes.admin import admin_bp
76	
77	    # Health probes (no version prefix — standard convention)
78	    app.register_blueprint(health_bp)
79	
80	    v1 = "/api/v1"
81	    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp, url_prefix=v1)

83	    app.register_blueprint(communities_bp, url_prefix=v1)
84	    app.register_blueprint(commission_bp, url_prefix=v1)
85	    app.register_blueprint(catalog_bp, url_prefix=v1)
86	    app.register_blueprint(search_bp, url_prefix=v1)
87	    app.register_blueprint(inventory_bp, url_prefix=v1)
88	    app.register_blueprint(messaging_bp, url_prefix=v1)
89	    app.register_blueprint(content_bp, url_prefix=v1)
90	    app.register_blueprint(templates_bp, url_prefix=v1)
91	    app.register_blueprint(admin_bp, url_prefix=v1)

97	    if not scheduler.running:
98	        scheduler.start()

1	"""
2	WebSocket / STOMP messaging endpoints.
3	Flask-SocketIO is used as the STOMP-compatible transport.
4	Authentication uses the Bearer token from the STOMP CONNECT frame headers.
5	"""
6	import hashlib
7	from datetime import datetime, timezone
8	
9	from flask_socketio import emit, join_room, disconnect
10	from flask import request
11	
12	from app.extensions import socketio, db
13	from app.services.messaging_service import MessagingService
14	
15	
16	# sid → (user, token_hash) mapping; populated on connect, cleaned up on disconnect
17	_sid_user_map: dict = {}
18	
19	
20	@socketio.on("connect", namespace="/ws/messaging")
21	def on_connect(auth):
22	    token = (auth or {}).get("token", "")
23	    user = _load_user(token)
24	    if user is None:
25	        disconnect()
26	        return
27	    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
28	    _sid_user_map[request.sid] = (user, token_hash)
29	    # Join personal room for direct message delivery
30	    join_room(f"user_{user.user_id}")
31	    # Join community rooms for group message delivery
32	    from app.models.community import CommunityMember
33	    memberships = CommunityMember.query.filter_by(
34	        user_id=user.user_id, left_at=None
35	    ).all()
36	    for m in memberships:
37	        join_room(f"community_{m.community_id}")
38	
39	
40	@socketio.on("disconnect", namespace="/ws/messaging")
41	def on_disconnect():
42	    _sid_user_map.pop(request.sid, None)
43	
44	
45	def _get_authenticated_user():
46	    """Return the user for the current sid, re-validating the session token."""
47	    entry = _sid_user_map.get(request.sid)
48	    if entry is None:
49	        return None
50	    user, token_hash = entry
51	    from app.models.user import Session
52	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
53	    if session is None:
54	        return None
55	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
56	        _sid_user_map.pop(request.sid, None)
57	        disconnect()
58	        return None
59	    return user
60	
61	
62	@socketio.on("direct", namespace="/ws/messaging")
63	def on_direct(data):
64	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
65	    if user is None:
66	        return
67	    data["correlation_id"] = getattr(__import__("flask").g, "correlation_id", "")
68	    msg = MessagingService.send_message(data, sender=user)
69	    emit("message", msg.to_dict(), room=f"user_{data.get('recipient_id')}")
70	
71	
72	@socketio.on("group", namespace="/ws/messaging")
73	def on_group(data):
74	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
75	    if user is None:
76	        return
77	    msg = MessagingService.send_message(data, sender=user)
78	    emit("message", msg.to_dict(), room=f"community_{data.get('group_id')}")
79	
80	
81	@socketio.on("receipt", namespace="/ws/messaging")
82	def on_receipt(data):
83	    user = _get_authenticated_user()
84	    if user is None:
85	        return
86	    MessagingService.update_receipt(data["message_id"], data["status"], user=user)
87	
88	
89	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
90	# Helpers
91	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
92	
93	def _load_user(token: str):
94	    """Load user from raw token string without touching the HTTP request object."""
95	    if not token:
96	        return None
97	    token_hash = hashlib.sha256(token.encode()).hexdigest()
98	    from app.models.user import Session, User
99	    session = db.session.get(Session, token_hash)
100	    if session is None:
101	        return None
102	    if session.expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) < datetime.now(timezone.utc):
103	        return None
104	    user = db.session.get(User, session.user_id)
105	    if user is None or user.deleted_at is not None:
106	        return None
107	    return user
108

1	"""
2	API functional tests for messaging REST endpoints.
3	
4	Covers: send direct/group messages, message queuing, delivery/read receipts,
5	        status regression enforcement, invalid inputs, and auth requirements.
6	All tests use the Flask test client against /api/v1/messages/* endpoints.
7	"""
8	import uuid
9	import pytest
10	
11	BASE = "/api/v1"
12	
13	
14	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	# Helpers
16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	
18	def _register_and_login(client, role="Member"):
19	    username = f"user_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
20	    password = "ValidPass1234!"
21	    reg = client.post(f"{BASE}/auth/register", json={
22	        "username": username,
23	        "password": password,
24	        "role": role,
25	    })
26	    user_id = reg.json["user_id"]
27	    token = client.post(f"{BASE}/auth/login", json={
28	        "username": username,
29	        "password": password,
30	    }).json["token"]
31	    return user_id, {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
32	
33	
34	def _send_direct(client, sender_headers, recipient_id, body="Hello!"):
35	    return client.post(f"{BASE}/messages", json={
36	        "type": "text",
37	        "recipient_id": recipient_id,
38	        "body": body,
39	    }, headers=sender_headers)
40	
41	
42	def _create_community(client, headers):
43	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/communities", json={
44	        "name": f"Comm_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
45	        "address_line1": "1 Main St",
46	        "city": "Austin",
47	        "state": "TX",
48	        "zip": "78701",
49	    }, headers=headers)
50	    assert resp.status_code == 201
51	    return resp.json["community_id"]
52	
53	
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	# Send message
56	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	
58	def test_send_direct_201(client, auth_headers):
59	    """POST /messages with type/recipient_id/body returns 201 with expected fields."""
60	    recip_id, _ = _register_and_login(client)
61	    resp = _send_direct(client, auth_headers, recip_id)
62	    assert resp.status_code == 201
63	    data = resp.json
64	    assert "message_id" in data
65	    assert "sender_id" in data
66	    assert "recipient_id" in data
67	    assert "sent_at" in data
68	    assert data["recipient_id"] == recip_id
69	
70	
71	def test_send_requires_auth_401(client):
72	    """POST /messages without a token returns 401."""
73	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages", json={
74	        "type": "text",
75	        "body": "no auth",
76	    })
77	    assert resp.status_code == 401
78	
79	
80	def test_send_invalid_type_400(client, auth_headers):
81	    """POST /messages with type=voice returns 400 with error=invalid_message_type."""
82	    recip_id, _ = _register_and_login(client)
83	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages", json={
84	        "type": "voice",
85	        "recipient_id": recip_id,
86	        "body": "hi",
87	    }, headers=auth_headers)
88	    assert resp.status_code == 400
89	    assert resp.json["error"] == "invalid_message_type"
90	
91	
92	def test_send_both_targets_400(client, auth_headers):
93	    """Providing both recipient_id and group_id returns 400 with error=ambiguous_target."""
94	    recip_id, _ = _register_and_login(client)
95	    group_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
96	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages", json={
97	        "type": "text",
98	        "recipient_id": recip_id,
99	        "group_id": group_id,
100	        "body": "ambiguous",
101	    }, headers=auth_headers)
102	    assert resp.status_code == 400
103	    assert resp.json["error"] == "ambiguous_target"
104	
105	
106	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107	# Queue polling
108	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
109	
110	def test_get_queued_200(client, auth_headers):
111	    """Recipient GET /messages returns 200 and the array contains the sent message."""
112	    recip_id, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
113	    _send_direct(client, auth_headers, recip_id, body="queue_test_body")
114	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/messages", headers=recip_headers)
115	    assert resp.status_code == 200
116	    msgs = resp.json
117	    assert isinstance(msgs, list)
118	    assert any(m["body"] == "queue_test_body" for m in msgs)
119	
120	
121	def test_sender_queue_empty(client, auth_headers):
122	    """Sender GET /messages does not see the messages they sent (no receipt for sender)."""
123	    recip_id, _ = _register_and_login(client)
124	    _send_direct(client, auth_headers, recip_id, body="sender_check_body")
125	    resp = client.get(f"{BASE}/messages", headers=auth_headers)
126	    assert resp.status_code == 200
127	    assert not any(m.get("body") == "sender_check_body" for m in resp.json)
128	
129	
130	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	# Receipts
132	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	
134	def test_receipt_delivered_200(client, auth_headers):
135	    """POST /messages/{id}/receipt with status=delivered returns 200."""
136	    recip_id, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
137	    msg_id = _send_direct(client, auth_headers, recip_id).json["message_id"]
138	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages/{msg_id}/receipt",
139	                       json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=recip_headers)
140	    assert resp.status_code == 200
141	
142	
143	def test_receipt_read_200(client, auth_headers):
144	    """POST /messages/{id}/receipt with status=read returns 200."""
145	    recip_id, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
146	    msg_id = _send_direct(client, auth_headers, recip_id).json["message_id"]
147	    client.post(f"{BASE}/messages/{msg_id}/receipt",
148	                json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=recip_headers)
149	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages/{msg_id}/receipt",
150	                       json={"status": "read"}, headers=recip_headers)
151	    assert resp.status_code == 200
152	
153	
154	def test_receipt_backward_409(client, auth_headers):
155	    """Marking a message delivered, then delivered again (regression) returns 409 with error=status_regression."""
156	    recip_id, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
157	    msg_id = _send_direct(client, auth_headers, recip_id).json["message_id"]
158	    # Advance to read
159	    client.post(f"{BASE}/messages/{msg_id}/receipt",
160	                json={"status": "read"}, headers=recip_headers)
161	    # Try to go back to delivered (regression)
162	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages/{msg_id}/receipt",
163	                       json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=recip_headers)
164	    assert resp.status_code == 409
165	    assert resp.json["error"] == "status_regression"
166	
167	
168	def test_receipt_invalid_status_400(client, auth_headers):
169	    """POST /messages/{id}/receipt with status=pending returns 400."""
170	    recip_id, recip_headers = _register_and_login(client)
171	    msg_id = _send_direct(client, auth_headers, recip_id).json["message_id"]
172	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages/{msg_id}/receipt",
173	                       json={"status": "pending"}, headers=recip_headers)
174	    assert resp.status_code == 400
175	
176	
177	def test_receipt_not_found_404(client, auth_headers):
178	    """POST /messages/{random_id}/receipt returns 404."""
179	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages/{uuid.uuid4()}/receipt",
180	                       json={"status": "delivered"}, headers=auth_headers)
181	    assert resp.status_code == 404
182	
183	
184	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
185	# Group messages
186	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
187	
188	def test_group_message_201(client, auth_headers, member_headers):
189	    """POST /messages with group_id after joining community returns 201."""
190	    community_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
191	    # Both sender (admin) and member must join; sender membership is enforced
192	    client.post(f"{BASE}/communities/{community_id}/members", headers=auth_headers)
193	    client.post(f"{BASE}/communities/{community_id}/members", headers=member_headers)
194	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages", json={
195	        "type": "text",
196	        "group_id": community_id,
197	        "body": "Group hello",
198	    }, headers=auth_headers)
199	    assert resp.status_code == 201
200	
201	
202	def test_group_message_non_member_403(client, auth_headers):
203	    """Sending a group message without being a community member returns 403."""
204	    community_id = _create_community(client, auth_headers)
205	    # Sender does NOT join the community
206	    resp = client.post(f"{BASE}/messages", json={
207	        "type": "text",
208	        "group_id": community_id,
209	        "body": "Non-member attempt",
210	    }, headers=auth_headers)
211	    assert resp.status_code == 403
212

1	"""
2	API functional tests for WebSocket/STOMP messaging (WS-1 gap fix).
3	
4	Uses flask_socketio.SocketIOTestClient to exercise the /ws/messaging namespace
5	directly — no HTTP; real event handlers, real DB, real service calls.
6	
7	All WebSocket operations (connect, emit, receive) run inside
8	``with app.app_context()`` because Flask-SocketIO's test client
9	executes event handlers synchronously in the same thread; the app context
10	keeps the SQLAlchemy session alive so handlers can see committed rows.
11	
12	The tests reuse the session-scoped ``app`` fixture from conftest.py to avoid
13	calling socketio.init_app() more than once per pytest session.
14	
15	Covered:
16	  - connect with valid token → is_connected True
17	  - connect with invalid/missing token → rejected (not connected)
18	  - on_direct: send direct message → recipient's room receives 'message' event
19	  - on_group: send group message → community room receives 'message' event
20	  - on_receipt: acknowledge delivery/read → receipt status advanced in DB
21	  - disconnect: _sid_user_map cleaned up (no stale entry)
22	  - token expiry mid-connection: expired token → disconnect on next event
23	"""
24	import uuid
25	import hashlib
26	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
27	
28	import pytest
29	
30	from app.extensions import db as _db, socketio
31	from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
32	from app.services.community_service import CommunityService
33	from app.models.user import Session
34	from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt
35	
36	
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	# Helpers
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	
41	def _new_user(app, role="Member"):
42	    """Register + login a fresh user; return (user_id_str, raw_token)."""
43	    suffix = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
44	    username = f"ws_{suffix}"
45	    with app.app_context():
46	        AuthService.register(username, "WsTestPass12!", role=role)
47	        result = AuthService.login(username, "WsTestPass12!")
48	    return result["user_id"], result["token"]
49	
50	
51	def _connect(app, token):
52	    """Return a connected SocketIOTestClient for /ws/messaging.
53	    Must be called within an active app_context."""
54	    return socketio.test_client(
55	        app, namespace="/ws/messaging", auth={"token": token}
56	    )
57	
58	
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	# Connection / authentication
61	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	class TestWsConnect:
64	
65	    def test_connect_valid_token(self, app):
66	        """A valid Bearer token results in a successful WebSocket connection."""
67	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
68	        with app.app_context():
69	            ws = _connect(app, tok_a)
70	            assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
71	            ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
72	
73	    def test_connect_invalid_token_rejected(self, app):
74	        """An invalid token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
75	        with app.app_context():
76	            ws = _connect(app, "notarealtoken123456")
77	            assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
78	
79	    def test_connect_empty_token_rejected(self, app):
80	        """An empty token causes the server to disconnect immediately."""
81	        with app.app_context():
82	            ws = _connect(app, "")
83	            assert not ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
84	
85	    def test_disconnect_cleans_up(self, app):
86	        """After explicit disconnect, _sid_user_map no longer has an entry for the user."""
87	        from app import websocket as ws_mod
88	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
89	        with app.app_context():
90	            ws = _connect(app, tok_a)
91	            assert ws.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
92	            assert any(
93	                str(u.user_id) == uid_a
94	                for u, _ in ws_mod._sid_user_map.values()
95	            ), f"User {uid_a} not found in sid_map"
96	            ws.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
97	            assert not any(
98	                str(u.user_id) == uid_a
99	                for u, _ in ws_mod._sid_user_map.values()
100	            ), f"User {uid_a} still in sid_map after disconnect"
101	
102	
103	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
104	# Direct messaging (on_direct)
105	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
106	
107	class TestWsDirect:
108	
109	    def test_direct_message_emitted_to_recipient_room(self, app):
110	        """Sending 'direct' from user A delivers a 'message' event to user B's socket."""
111	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
112	        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(app)
113	
114	        with app.app_context():
115	            ws_a = _connect(app, tok_a)
116	            ws_b = _connect(app, tok_b)
117	
118	            ws_a.emit(
119	                "direct",
120	                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Hello from A"},
121	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
122	            )
123	
124	            received_b = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
125	            assert any(evt["name"] == "message" for evt in received_b), \
126	                f"Recipient did not receive 'message' event; got: {received_b}"
127	
128	            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
129	            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
130	
131	    def test_direct_message_persisted_as_receipt(self, app):
132	        """After a 'direct' event, a MessageReceipt row is written with status='sent'."""
133	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
134	        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(app)
135	
136	        with app.app_context():
137	            ws_a = _connect(app, tok_a)
138	
139	            ws_a.emit(
140	                "direct",
141	                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Persist check"},
142	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
143	            )
144	
145	            receipts = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).all()
146	            assert len(receipts) >= 1
147	            assert receipts[-1].status == "sent"
148	
149	            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
150	
151	    def test_direct_message_body_in_event(self, app):
152	        """The emitted 'message' event payload contains the sent body and sender_id."""
153	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
154	        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(app)
155	
156	        with app.app_context():
157	            ws_b = _connect(app, tok_b)
158	            ws_a = _connect(app, tok_a)
159	
160	            ws_a.emit(
161	                "direct",
162	                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "unique_body_xyz"},
163	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
164	            )
165	
166	            events = ws_b.get_received("/ws/messaging")
167	            message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
168	            assert message_events, "No 'message' event received"
169	            payload = message_events[-1]["args"]
170	            if isinstance(payload, list):
171	                payload = payload[0]
172	            assert payload.get("body") == "unique_body_xyz"
173	            assert payload.get("sender_id") == uid_a
174	
175	            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
176	            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
177	
178	
179	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	# Group messaging (on_group)
181	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	
183	class TestWsGroup:
184	
185	    def test_group_message_delivered_to_community_room(self, app):
186	        """Sending 'group' emits a 'message' event to the community room.
187	        A member connected before the send receives the event."""
188	        uid_admin, tok_admin = _new_user(app, role="Administrator")
189	        uid_member, tok_member = _new_user(app)
190	
191	        with app.app_context():
192	            comm = CommunityService.create({
193	                "name": f"WS Comm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
194	                "address_line1": "1 Test St",
195	                "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701",
196	            })
197	            comm_id = str(comm.community_id)
198	
199	            from app.models.user import User
200	            admin_obj = _db.session.get(User, uid_admin)
201	            member_obj = _db.session.get(User, uid_member)
202	            # Both sender and recipient must be active community members
203	            CommunityService.join_community(comm_id, admin_obj)
204	            CommunityService.join_community(comm_id, member_obj)
205	
206	            ws_admin = _connect(app, tok_admin)
207	            ws_member = _connect(app, tok_member)
208	
209	            ws_admin.emit(
210	                "group",
211	                {"type": "text", "group_id": comm_id, "body": "Group announcement"},
212	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
213	            )
214	
215	            events = ws_member.get_received("/ws/messaging")
216	            message_events = [e for e in events if e["name"] == "message"]
217	            assert message_events, \
218	                f"Community member did not receive group 'message'; got: {events}"
219	
220	            ws_admin.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
221	            ws_member.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
222	
223	
224	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
225	# Receipt acknowledgement (on_receipt)
226	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
227	
228	class TestWsReceipt:
229	
230	    def test_receipt_delivered_via_ws(self, app):
231	        """Sending 'receipt' with status='delivered' advances the MessageReceipt in DB."""
232	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
233	        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(app)
234	
235	        with app.app_context():
236	            ws_a = _connect(app, tok_a)
237	            ws_b = _connect(app, tok_b)
238	
239	            ws_a.emit(
240	                "direct",
241	                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Receipt test"},
242	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
243	            )
244	
245	            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
246	                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
247	            ).first()
248	            assert receipt is not None
249	            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
250	            rid = receipt.receipt_id
251	
252	            ws_b.emit(
253	                "receipt",
254	                {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"},
255	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
256	            )
257	
258	            _db.session.expire(receipt)
259	            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
260	            assert updated.status == "delivered"
261	
262	            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
263	            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
264	
265	    def test_receipt_read_via_ws(self, app):
266	        """Sending 'receipt' with status='read' after 'delivered' advances to read."""
267	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
268	        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(app)
269	
270	        with app.app_context():
271	            ws_a = _connect(app, tok_a)
272	            ws_b = _connect(app, tok_b)
273	
274	            ws_a.emit(
275	                "direct",
276	                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Read test"},
277	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
278	            )
279	
280	            receipt = MessageReceipt.query.filter_by(recipient_id=uid_b).order_by(
281	                MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()
282	            ).first()
283	            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
284	            rid = receipt.receipt_id
285	
286	            ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")
287	            ws_b.emit("receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "read"}, namespace="/ws/messaging")
288	
289	            _db.session.expire(receipt)
290	            updated = _db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
291	            assert updated.status == "read"
292	
293	            ws_a.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
294	            ws_b.disconnect("/ws/messaging")
295	
296	
297	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
298	# Token expiry mid-connection (RISK-003)
299	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
300	
301	class TestWsTokenExpiry:
302	
303	    def test_expired_token_disconnects_on_next_event(self, app):
304	        """A connection whose session has expired is disconnected on the next event."""
305	        uid_a, tok_a = _new_user(app)
306	        uid_b, tok_b = _new_user(app)
307	
308	        with app.app_context():
309	            ws_a = _connect(app, tok_a)
310	            assert ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
311	
312	            # Artificially expire the session in DB
313	            h = hashlib.sha256(tok_a.encode()).hexdigest()
314	            session = _db.session.get(Session, h)
315	            session.expires_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(seconds=1)
316	            _db.session.commit()
317	
318	            # Next event should trigger re-validation, find expired session, disconnect
319	            ws_a.emit(
320	                "direct",
321	                {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "Should be rejected"},
322	                namespace="/ws/messaging",
323	            )
324	
325	            assert not ws_a.is_connected("/ws/messaging")
326

1	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g
2	from app.middleware.auth import require_auth
3	from app.middleware.rbac import require_roles
4	from app.services.commission_service import CommissionService
5	
6	commission_bp = Blueprint("commission", __name__)
7	
8	
9	@commission_bp.post("/communities/<community_id>/commission-rules")
10	@require_auth
11	@require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
12	def create_rule(community_id):
13	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
14	    rule = CommissionService.create_rule(community_id, data)
15	    return jsonify(rule.to_dict()), 201
16	
17	
18	@commission_bp.get("/communities/<community_id>/commission-rules")
19	@require_auth
20	def list_rules(community_id):
21	    # Group Leaders may only view rules for their own community
22	    CommissionService.assert_can_read(community_id, g.current_user)
23	    return jsonify(CommissionService.list_rules(community_id))
24	
25	
26	@commission_bp.patch("/communities/<community_id>/commission-rules/<rule_id>")
27	@require_auth
28	@require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
29	def update_rule(community_id, rule_id):
30	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
31	    rule = CommissionService.update_rule(community_id, rule_id, data)
32	    return jsonify(rule.to_dict())
33	
34	
35	@commission_bp.delete("/communities/<community_id>/commission-rules/<rule_id>")
36	@require_auth
37	@require_roles("Administrator")
38	def delete_rule(community_id, rule_id):
39	    CommissionService.delete_rule(community_id, rule_id)
40	    return "", 204
41	
42	
43	# --- Settlements ---
44	
45	@commission_bp.post("/settlements")
46	@require_auth
47	@require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
48	def create_settlement():
49	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
50	    settlement, created = CommissionService.create_settlement(data, actor=g.current_user)
51	    # 201 on creation; 409 with existing settlement body on duplicate idempotency key
52	    return jsonify(settlement.to_dict()), 201 if created else 409
53	
54	
55	@commission_bp.get("/settlements/<settlement_id>")
56	@require_auth
57	def get_settlement(settlement_id):
58	    CommissionService.assert_can_read_settlement(settlement_id, g.current_user)
59	    settlement = CommissionService.get_settlement(settlement_id)
60	    return jsonify(settlement.to_dict())
61	
62	
63	@commission_bp.post("/settlements/<settlement_id>/disputes")
64	@require_auth
65	def file_dispute(settlement_id):
66	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
67	    dispute = CommissionService.file_dispute(settlement_id, data, actor=g.current_user)
68	    return jsonify(dispute.to_dict()), 201
69	
70	
71	@commission_bp.patch("/settlements/<settlement_id>/disputes/<dispute_id>")
72	@require_auth
73	@require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
74	def resolve_dispute(settlement_id, dispute_id):
75	    data = request.get_json(force=True) or {}
76	    dispute = CommissionService.resolve_dispute(settlement_id, dispute_id, data)
77	    return jsonify(dispute.to_dict())
78	
79	
80	@commission_bp.post("/settlements/<settlement_id>/finalize")
81	@require_auth
82	@require_roles("Administrator", "Operations Manager")
83	def finalize_settlement(settlement_id):
84	    settlement = CommissionService.finalize(settlement_id, actor=g.current_user)
85	    return jsonify(settlement.to_dict())

    def _get_or_create_lot(sku_id, warehouse_id, bin_id, lot_number, costing_method) -> InventoryLot:
        # SKU-level costing policy: first receipt for a SKU locks the method for all lots/warehouses
        if costing_method:
            policy = db.session.get(SkuCostingPolicy, sku_id)
            if policy is None:
                policy = SkuCostingPolicy(sku_id=sku_id, costing_method=costing_method)
                db.session.add(policy)
                db.session.flush()
            elif policy.costing_method != costing_method:
                raise UnprocessableError(
                    "costing_method_locked",
                    f"SKU costing method is locked to '{policy.costing_method}' "
                    f"from the first transaction",
                )

        lot = InventoryLot.query.filter_by(
            sku_id=sku_id, warehouse_id=warehouse_id, bin_id=bin_id, lot_number=lot_number
        ).first()
        if lot is None:
            from app.models.catalog import Product

            prod = db.session.get(Product, sku_id)
            threshold = int(prod.safety_stock_threshold or 0) if prod else 0
            lot = InventoryLot(
                sku_id=sku_id, warehouse_id=warehouse_id, bin_id=bin_id,
                lot_number=lot_number, costing_method=costing_method,
                safety_stock_threshold=threshold,
            )
            db.session.add(lot)
            db.session.flush()
        elif costing_method and lot.costing_method != costing_method:
            # Per-lot immutability (also enforced by DB trigger)
            raise UnprocessableError("costing_method_locked",
                                     "Costing method cannot be changed after first transaction")
        return lot"}

128	    """Category-specific rule takes precedence over community default."""
129	    from app.services.commission_service import CommissionService
130	    from app import create_app
131	    cid = _community(client, auth_headers)
132	    # Community default at 5%
133	    _rule(client, auth_headers, cid, rate=5.0)
134	    # Category rule at 9%
135	    _rule(client, auth_headers, cid, rate=9.0, product_category="Electronics")
136	
137	    with client.application.app_context():
138	        # Category match → 9%
139	        assert CommissionService.resolve_rate(cid, "Electronics") == 9.0
140	        # Other category → community default 5%

142	        # No category → community default 5%
143	        assert CommissionService.resolve_rate(cid) == 5.0

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Settlement audit trail
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_create_settlement_writes_audit_log(client, auth_headers):
    """create_settlement must append an AuditLog row with action_type='settlement'."""
    from app.models.audit import AuditLog
    cid = _community(client, auth_headers)
    resp = _settlement(client, auth_headers, cid)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    sid = resp.json["settlement_id"]
    with client.application.app_context():
        log = AuditLog.query.filter_by(
            action_type="settlement", target_type="settlement_run", target_id=sid
        ).first()
        assert log is not None, "AuditLog row missing for create_settlement"
        import json
        after = json.loads(log.after_state)
        assert after["status"] == "pending"


def test_file_dispute_writes_audit_log(client, auth_headers):
    """file_dispute must append an AuditLog row with action_type='settlement'."""
    from app.models.audit import AuditLog
    cid = _community(client, auth_headers)
    sid = _settlement(client, auth_headers, cid).json["settlement_id"]
    dispute_resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/settlements/{sid}/disputes",
        json={"reason": "Audit test", "disputed_amount": 10.0},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert dispute_resp.status_code == 201
    did = dispute_resp.json["dispute_id"]
    with client.application.app_context():
        log = AuditLog.query.filter_by(
            action_type="settlement", target_type="settlement_dispute", target_id=did
        ).first()
        assert log is not None, "AuditLog row missing for file_dispute"


def test_resolve_dispute_writes_audit_log(client, auth_headers):
    """resolve_dispute must append an AuditLog row with action_type='settlement'."""
    from app.models.audit import AuditLog
    cid = _community(client, auth_headers)
    sid = _settlement(client, auth_headers, cid).json["settlement_id"]
    did = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/settlements/{sid}/disputes",
        json={"reason": "Audit test", "disputed_amount": 5.0},
        headers=auth_headers,
    ).json["dispute_id"]
    client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/settlements/{sid}/disputes/{did}",
        json={"resolution": "resolved", "notes": "OK"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    with client.application.app_context():
        logs = AuditLog.query.filter_by(
            action_type="settlement", target_type="settlement_dispute", target_id=did
        ).all()
        # Two rows expected: file + resolve
        assert len(logs) == 2, f"Expected 2 audit rows for dispute {did}, got {len(logs)}"
        resolutions = [json.loads(l.after_state)["status"] for l in logs
                       if l.after_state and "status" in json.loads(l.after_state)]
        assert "resolved" in resolutions


def test_finalize_writes_audit_log(client, auth_headers):
    """finalize must append an AuditLog row with action_type='settlement' and status=completed."""
    from app.models.audit import AuditLog
    import json
    cid = _community(client, auth_headers)
    sid = _settlement(client, auth_headers, cid).json["settlement_id"]
    client.post(f"/api/v1/settlements/{sid}/finalize", headers=auth_headers)
    with client.application.app_context():
        logs = AuditLog.query.filter_by(
            action_type="settlement", target_type="settlement_run", target_id=sid
        ).all()
        assert len(logs) == 2, f"Expected 2 audit rows (create+finalize) for {sid}"
        statuses = [json.loads(l.after_state)["status"] for l in logs
                    if l.after_state and "status" in json.loads(l.after_state)]
        assert "completed" in statuses"}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Transaction history
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_list_transactions(client, auth_headers):
    import uuid
    sku_id = _create_product(client, auth_headers, f"INV-TXN-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}")
    wh_id = _create_warehouse(client, auth_headers)
    _receipt(client, auth_headers, sku_id, wh_id, qty=10)
    _issue(client, auth_headers, sku_id, wh_id, qty=3)

    resp = client.get(f"/api/v1/inventory/transactions?sku_id={sku_id}",
                      headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json["total"] == 2
    types = {t["type"] for t in resp.json["items"]}
    assert "receipt" in types
    assert "issue" in types


def test_list_transactions_type_filter(client, auth_headers):
    import uuid
    sku_id = _create_product(client, auth_headers, f"INV-TXNF-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}")

{"file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\API_tests\test_api_stomp.py", "content": """"
API functional tests for the STOMP WebSocket endpoint (/ws/stomp).

Uses a mock WebSocket to exercise _handle_stomp_connection directly
(flask-sock's real WebSocket requires an HTTP upgrade; this approach
verifies the STOMP protocol state machine without needing a live server).

Covered:
  - CONNECT with valid token → CONNECTED frame
  - CONNECT with invalid token → ERROR frame
  - CONNECT with missing / empty token → ERROR frame
  - SUBSCRIBE → RECEIPT sent when receipt header present
  - SEND /app/direct → MESSAGE frame echoed back, message persisted
  - SEND /app/group → MESSAGE frame echoed back (after joining community)
  - SEND /app/receipt → receipt status advanced in DB
  - DISCONNECT → RECEIPT sent, loop exits
  - SEND before CONNECT → ERROR frame
"""
import json
import uuid
import pytest

from app.stomp_ws import _parse_frame, _build_frame, _handle_stomp_connection
from app.services.auth_service import AuthService
from app.services.community_service import CommunityService


_NUL = "\x00"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fake WebSocket for in-process testing
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class _FakeWs:
    """Minimal WebSocket stub consumed by _handle_stomp_connection."""

    def __init__(self, frames):
        self._in = list(frames)   # frames the "client" sends
        self.sent = []            # frames captured from server send()

    def receive(self, timeout=None):
        if not self._in:
            return None
        return self._in.pop(0)

    def send(self, data):
        self.sent.append(data)

    def _parsed_sent(self):
        return [_parse_frame(f) for f in self.sent]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_user(app, role="Member"):
    suffix = uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]
    username = f"stomp_{suffix}"
    with app.app_context():
        AuthService.register(username, "StompTest12!", role=role)
        result = AuthService.login(username, "StompTest12!")
    return result["user_id"], result["token"]


def _connect_frame(token):
    return _build_frame("CONNECT", {
        "accept-version": "1.2",
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
        "host": "localhost",
    })


def _subscribe_frame(destination, sub_id="sub-0", receipt="r-sub"):
    return _build_frame("SUBSCRIBE", {
        "id": sub_id,
        "destination": destination,
        "ack": "auto",
        "receipt": receipt,
    })


def _send_frame(destination, body_dict, receipt=None):
    hdrs = {"destination": destination, "content-type": "application/json"}
    if receipt:
        hdrs["receipt"] = receipt
    return _build_frame("SEND", hdrs, json.dumps(body_dict))


def _disconnect_frame(receipt="r-disc"):
    return _build_frame("DISCONNECT", {"receipt": receipt})


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Frame parsing / building unit tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def test_parse_frame_connect():
    raw = "CONNECT\naccept-version:1.2\nAuthorization:Bearer tok\n\n\x00"
    cmd, hdrs, body = _parse_frame(raw)
    assert cmd == "CONNECT"
    assert hdrs["Authorization"] == "Bearer tok"
    assert body == ""


def test_build_frame_connected():
    frame = _build_frame("CONNECTED", {"version": "1.2"}, "")
    assert frame.startswith("CONNECTED\n")
    assert "version:1.2" in frame
    assert frame.endswith(_NUL)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Connection / authentication
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompConnect:

    def test_connect_valid_token(self, app):
        uid, tok = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([_connect_frame(tok)])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        cmds = [f[0] for f in ws._parsed_sent()]
        assert "CONNECTED" in cmds, f"Expected CONNECTED frame, got: {cmds}"

    def test_connect_invalid_token(self, app):
        ws = _FakeWs([_connect_frame("notarealtoken")])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        cmds = [f[0] for f in ws._parsed_sent()]
        assert "ERROR" in cmds, f"Expected ERROR frame, got: {cmds}"
        assert "CONNECTED" not in cmds

    def test_connect_empty_token(self, app):
        frame = _build_frame("CONNECT", {"accept-version": "1.2", "host": "localhost"})
        ws = _FakeWs([frame])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        cmds = [f[0] for f in ws._parsed_sent()]
        assert "ERROR" in cmds

    def test_disconnect_sends_receipt(self, app):
        uid, tok = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([_connect_frame(tok), _disconnect_frame(receipt="r-99")])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        parsed = ws._parsed_sent()
        receipt_cmds = [f for f in parsed if f[0] == "RECEIPT"]
        assert receipt_cmds, "Expected RECEIPT on DISCONNECT"
        assert any(f[1].get("receipt-id") == "r-99" for f in receipt_cmds)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUBSCRIBE
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompSubscribe:

    def test_subscribe_sends_receipt(self, app):
        uid, tok = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok),
            _subscribe_frame("/user/queue/messages", receipt="r-sub-1"),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        parsed = ws._parsed_sent()
        receipt_ids = [f[1].get("receipt-id") for f in parsed if f[0] == "RECEIPT"]
        assert "r-sub-1" in receipt_ids, f"RECEIPT r-sub-1 not found in: {parsed}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND /app/direct
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompSendDirect:

    def test_send_direct_returns_message_frame(self, app):
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {
                "type": "text",
                "recipient_id": uid_b,
                "body": "Hello via STOMP",
            }, receipt="r-dir"),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        parsed = ws._parsed_sent()
        msg_frames = [f for f in parsed if f[0] == "MESSAGE"]
        assert msg_frames, f"Expected MESSAGE frame, got: {[f[0] for f in parsed]}"
        payload = json.loads(msg_frames[0][2])
        assert payload["body"] == "Hello via STOMP"
        assert payload["sender_id"] == uid_a

    def test_send_direct_persists_to_db(self, app):
        from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {
                "type": "text",
                "recipient_id": uid_b,
                "body": "Persist STOMP check",
            }),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
            from app.extensions import db
            receipts = db.session.query(MessageReceipt).filter_by(
                recipient_id=uid_b
            ).all()
        assert len(receipts) >= 1


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND /app/group
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompSendGroup:

    def test_send_group_returns_message_frame(self, app):
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app, role="Administrator")
        with app.app_context():
            comm = CommunityService.create({
                "name": f"StompComm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
                "address_line1": "1 STOMP St",
                "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701",
            })
            comm_id = str(comm.community_id)
            from app.extensions import db
            from app.models.user import User
            admin_obj = db.session.get(User, uid_a)
            CommunityService.join_community(comm_id, admin_obj)

        ws = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/group", {
                "type": "text",
                "group_id": comm_id,
                "body": "Group STOMP message",
            }, receipt="r-grp"),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        parsed = ws._parsed_sent()
        msg_frames = [f for f in parsed if f[0] == "MESSAGE"]
        assert msg_frames, f"Expected MESSAGE frame, got: {[f[0] for f in parsed]}"
        payload = json.loads(msg_frames[0][2])
        assert payload["body"] == "Group STOMP message"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND /app/receipt
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompReceipt:

    def test_send_receipt_advances_status(self, app):
        from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)

        # Send a direct message via STOMP to create a DB receipt
        ws_send = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "receipt test"}),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws_send, app)
            from app.extensions import db
            receipt = db.session.query(MessageReceipt).filter_by(
                recipient_id=uid_b
            ).order_by(MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()).first()
            assert receipt is not None
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

        # Now advance status via STOMP receipt frame
        ws_ack = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_b),
            _send_frame("/app/receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"}),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws_ack, app)
            from app.extensions import db
            updated = db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "delivered"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND before CONNECT
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompNoAuth:

    def test_send_without_connect_returns_error(self, app):
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "nope"}),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        cmds = [f[0] for f in ws._parsed_sent()]
        assert "ERROR" in cmds, f"Expected ERROR before auth, got: {cmds}"
"}

            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        parsed = ws._parsed_sent()
        receipt_ids = [f[1].get("receipt-id") for f in parsed if f[0] == "RECEIPT"]
        assert "r-sub-1" in receipt_ids, f"RECEIPT r-sub-1 not found in: {parsed}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND /app/direct
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompSendDirect:

    def test_send_direct_returns_message_frame(self, app):
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {
                "type": "text",
                "recipient_id": uid_b,
                "body": "Hello via STOMP",
            }, receipt="r-dir"),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        parsed = ws._parsed_sent()
        msg_frames = [f for f in parsed if f[0] == "MESSAGE"]
        assert msg_frames, f"Expected MESSAGE frame, got: {[f[0] for f in parsed]}"
        payload = json.loads(msg_frames[0][2])
        assert payload["body"] == "Hello via STOMP"
        assert payload["sender_id"] == uid_a

    def test_send_direct_persists_to_db(self, app):
        from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {
                "type": "text",
                "recipient_id": uid_b,
                "body": "Persist STOMP check",
            }),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
            from app.extensions import db
            receipts = db.session.query(MessageReceipt).filter_by(
                recipient_id=uid_b
            ).all()
        assert len(receipts) >= 1


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND /app/group
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompSendGroup:

    def test_send_group_returns_message_frame(self, app):
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app, role="Administrator")
        with app.app_context():
            comm = CommunityService.create({
                "name": f"StompComm {uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}",
                "address_line1": "1 STOMP St",
                "city": "Austin", "state": "TX", "zip": "78701",
            })
            comm_id = str(comm.community_id)
            from app.extensions import db
            from app.models.user import User
            admin_obj = db.session.get(User, uid_a)
            CommunityService.join_community(comm_id, admin_obj)

        ws = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/group", {
                "type": "text",
                "group_id": comm_id,
                "body": "Group STOMP message",
            }, receipt="r-grp"),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        parsed = ws._parsed_sent()
        msg_frames = [f for f in parsed if f[0] == "MESSAGE"]
        assert msg_frames, f"Expected MESSAGE frame, got: {[f[0] for f in parsed]}"
        payload = json.loads(msg_frames[0][2])
        assert payload["body"] == "Group STOMP message"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND /app/receipt
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompReceipt:

    def test_send_receipt_advances_status(self, app):
        from app.models.messaging import MessageReceipt
        uid_a, tok_a = _make_user(app)
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)

        # Send a direct message via STOMP to create a DB receipt
        ws_send = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_a),
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "receipt test"}),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws_send, app)
            from app.extensions import db
            receipt = db.session.query(MessageReceipt).filter_by(
                recipient_id=uid_b
            ).order_by(MessageReceipt.updated_at.desc()).first()
            assert receipt is not None
            msg_id = str(receipt.message_id)
            rid = receipt.receipt_id

        # Now advance status via STOMP receipt frame
        ws_ack = _FakeWs([
            _connect_frame(tok_b),
            _send_frame("/app/receipt", {"message_id": msg_id, "status": "delivered"}),
            _disconnect_frame(),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws_ack, app)
            from app.extensions import db
            updated = db.session.get(MessageReceipt, rid)
            assert updated.status == "delivered"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SEND before CONNECT
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestStompNoAuth:

    def test_send_without_connect_returns_error(self, app):
        uid_b, tok_b = _make_user(app)
        ws = _FakeWs([
            _send_frame("/app/direct", {"type": "text", "recipient_id": uid_b, "body": "nope"}),
        ])
        with app.app_context():
            _handle_stomp_connection(ws, app)
        cmds = [f[0] for f in ws._parsed_sent()]
        assert "ERROR" in cmds, f"Expected ERROR before auth, got: {cmds}"
"}

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "C:\TASK-105\repo\README.md", "old_string": "## Transport note — WebSocket / STOMP vs Socket.IO", "new_string": "## STOMP WebSocket endpoint (`/ws/stomp`)

A standards-compliant STOMP 1.2 endpoint is available at `/ws/stomp` (via `flask-sock`).

### Connecting

```
CONNECT
accept-version:1.2
Authorization:Bearer <token>
host:localhost

\0
```

Server responds with `CONNECTED` on success, `ERROR` on invalid/expired token.

### Sending messages

| Destination | Body JSON | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `/app/direct` | `{"type":"text","recipient_id":"<uid>","body":"..."}` | Direct message |
| `/app/group` | `{"type":"text","group_id":"<community_id>","body":"..."}` | Community group message |
| `/app/receipt` | `{"message_id":"<id>","status":"delivered\|read"}` | Advance receipt status |

### Subscribing (read-only)

| Destination | Content |
|---|---|
| `/user/queue/messages` | Personal direct and group messages |
| `/topic/community.<id>` | Community-scoped messages |

The server echoes a `MESSAGE` frame back to the sender's connection for each successful `SEND`.

---

## Transport note — WebSocket / STOMP vs Socket.IO"}

content.text: The file C:\TASK-105\repo\README.md has been updated successfully.

All three HIGH fixes are implemented and 409 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

### Fix 1: Settlement Audit Trails (`app/services/commission_service.py`)

Added `AuditLog` entries (same transaction as the mutation) for all four operations:
- **`create_settlement`** — logs `status=pending`, amounts, community_id
- **`file_dispute`** — logs `status=open`, reason, disputed_amount
- **`resolve_dispute`** — logs before/after status; also added `actor` parameter (passed from route)
- **`finalize`** — logs `pending→completed`

All use `action_type="settlement"`, include `actor_id` and `correlation_id`.

Tests added in `tests/test_commission.py` verify AuditLog rows for all four operations.

---

### Fix 2: SKU-level Costing Method Immutability

- **`app/models/inventory.py`** — Added `SkuCostingPolicy` model (`sku_id PK`, `costing_method`, `locked_at`)
- **`migrations/versions/0007_sku_costing_policy.py`** — Creates the `sku_costing_policies` table
- **`app/services/inventory_service.py`** — `_get_or_create_lot()` now checks/creates `SkuCostingPolicy` before creating the lot; a different method for the same SKU raises `422 costing_method_locked`

Tests added in `tests/test_inventory.py`: policy created on first receipt, blocked on mismatch, allowed when same method.

---

### Fix 3: STOMP WebSocket Endpoint (`/ws/stomp`)

- **`app/stomp_ws.py`** — Full STOMP 1.2 handler: frame parser/serialiser, `CONNECT/STOMP/SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE/SEND/DISCONNECT` support, auth from `Authorization: Bearer` header, routes SEND frames to `MessagingService`
- **`requirements.txt`** — Added `flask-sock==0.7.0`
- **`app/__init__.py`** — Calls `register_stomp(app)` in factory
- **`README.md`** — Documents the STOMP endpoint, destinations, and frame format

Tests in `API_tests/test_api_stomp.py` cover: valid/invalid CONNECT, SUBSCRIBE receipt, SEND direct/group/receipt happy paths, persist-to-DB, SEND-before-CONNECT error.
